issue_title: Properly define APIException
issue_body:
Custom exception that inherits from Exception must call the super().init() method
```python
        super(APIException, self).__init__(message)
```

This creates minor errors in some case, like raising an APIException while in a Celery task.

issue_title: Ability to have waiters know about prior runs result
issue_body:
I am using tenacity in a slack bot that is being used; and I'm using it around the code that sends all the things to slack apis; those apis sometimes report back issues that should be retried (as would be expected).

One of the interesting ones is when they send back the response 'rate_limited' 👍 
   https://api.slack.com/docs/rate-limits

When they do this they will send back a 'Retry-After' header (which there python client now includes in there custom exception class) so it would be nice to have a waiter class that can be given the prior result (or exception) and adjust the amount of time waited based on that result.

Right now it seems that waiters just get a `previous_attempt_number, delay_since_first_attempt` which isn't quite enough to extract the suggested  'Retry-After' header from :(

issue_title: Inconsistent formatting with --format=pep8
issue_body:
I have noticed that the formatting for "--format pep8" is inconsistent, because
it will not have a type field (E) and number:

Given `t.py`:
```
 = meh
```

```
% pylama -l  mccabe,pep8,pyflakes,pep257 --format=pep8 t.py
t.py:1:2: E111 indentation is not a multiple of four [pep8]
t.py:1:2: E113 unexpected indentation [pep8]
t.py:1:1: unexpected indent [pyflakes]
```

This makes it more difficult to parse (e.g. using Vim's errorformat).

With --format=pylint:

```
% pylama -l  mccabe,pep8,pyflakes,pep257 --format=pylint t.py
t.py:1: [D] D100: Missing docstring in public module [pep257]
t.py:1: [E] Invalid syntax: = meh [mccabe]
t.py:1: [E] unexpected indent [pyflakes]
```


(The same file with the "pylint" format gets different errors, which appears to
be a separate issue?!  pep257 and mccabe is used here, but pep8 is not.
That happens when using pyflakes, which throws a SyntaxError from https://github.com/klen/pylama/blob/e9e926fca9c8ba388b5a140bc3f4c13a3dc76ddb/pylama/lint/pylama_pyflakes.py#L41, which causes pylama to abort.  It does not depend on the format, but in which order linters get run)

issue_title: 控制台二维码输出和二维码图片输出两者不能同时进行
issue_body:
site-packages/itchat/components/login.py中get_QR方法中的代码如下：
def get_QR(self, uuid=None, enableCmdQR=False, picDir=None, qrCallback=None):
    uuid = uuid or self.uuid
    picDir = picDir or config.DEFAULT_QR
    qrStorage = io.BytesIO()
    qrCode = QRCode('https://login.weixin.qq.com/l/' + uuid)
    qrCode.png(qrStorage, scale=10)
    if hasattr(qrCallback, '__call__'):
        qrCallback(uuid=uuid, status='0', qrcode=qrStorage.getvalue())
    else:
        if enableCmdQR:
            utils.print_cmd_qr(qrCode.text(1), enableCmdQR=enableCmdQR)
        else:
            with open(picDir, 'wb') as f:
                f.write(qrStorage.getvalue())
            utils.print_qr(picDir)
    return qrStorage

当enableCmdQR有值时就不能输出二维码图片啦，可根据需要自行修改

issue_title: DotMap.copy does not return an independent copy of the content
issue_body:
Currently copy() returns a reference to self, which means you're not working on an unique instance when working on the instance returned by copy()

``` 
>>> from dotmap import DotMap
>>> d1 = DotMap({'s': 1})
>>> d2 = d1.copy()
>>> d1.x = 2
>>> d1
DotMap(s=1, x=2)
>>> d2 
DotMap(s=1, x=2)
```

issue_title: Missing validation of module/submodule revisions
issue_body:
In RFC6087, there's a statement under Lifecycle Management regarding the revision date of a module with respect to its submodules.

"If submodules are used, then the document containing the main module MUST be updated so that the main module revision date is equal or more recent than the revision date of any submodule that is (directly or indirectly) included by the main module."

pyang does not currently check for this which allows a module with an older revision than its submodules to pass validation with the --lint option

issue_title: modify operations not preserved through referral
issue_body:
When connecting to an LDAP server that returns a referral on modify, only the last operation is performed for an attribute. The same behavior is not observed when connecting directly to the master server, where all operations are performed.

The connection object is setup the same in both cases, with only the "hostname" in one case being the master and in the other case a slave (which returns a referral to the master)

    serverLDAP = ldap3.Server(host = hostname, port = 636, 
        use_ssl = True, connect_timeout = 3, get_info=ldap3.ALL)
    conn = ldap3.Connection(serverLDAP, lazy = False, raise_exceptions = True,
        authentication = ldap3.SIMPLE, check_names=True, user=userDN, password=password)
    conn.bind()

I then modify an object's description field, which has been manually reset to ["foo"] after each attempt.

    conn.modify("cn=my_test_group,ou=Groups,dc=domain,dc=local",
        {'description': [
            (ldap3.MODIFY_ADD, ["bar"]),
            (ldap3.MODIFY_DELETE, ["foo"])
    ]})

The expected behavior is of course that the description field should contain ["bar"] afterwards.
When making the change directly on the LDAP master, the audit logs the change as expected:

    dn: cn=my_test_group,ou=Groups,dc=domain,dc=local
    changetype: modify
    add: description
    description: bar
    -
    delete: description
    description: foo

But when making the change on the slave and following the referral the master audit logs the following:

    dn: cn=my_test_group,ou=Groups,dc=domain,dc=local
    changetype: modify
    delete: description
    description: foo

If I reverse the order of the two operations or add additional operations on the same object, it's still only the last operation that is actually performed.
The same behavior is seen when the attribute has multiple values before the modification.
If I attempt to modify two attributes at the same time (with more than one operation each) then only the last operation in each of the attributes is performed.

The test was done towards OpenLDAP/slapd v2.4.44, using python 3.5.0 and 3.6.1 with ldap3 v2.2.4.

issue_title: Test test_get_all_properties fails with given PYTHONHASHSEED
issue_body:
While exploring tox and testing in general, I found out that one particular test was failing unpredictably on my dev system, under Python 2.7.

I found a way of reproducing it by passing in a particular value for PYTHONHASHSEED.

I run the test with `trial  txdbus.test.test_client_against_native_bus.InterfaceTester.test_get_all_properties` which will only run if a DBUS session is up. So, on my dev VM with no GUI, I actually run `dbus-run-session trial  txdbus.test.test_client_against_native_bus.InterfaceTester.test_get_all_properties`. This runs successfully most of the time.

To force a failure, I need to set PYTHONHASHSEED to 3475844530 by running `PYTHONHASHSEED=3475844530 dbus-run-session trial  txdbus.test.test_client_against_native_bus.InterfaceTester.test_get_all_properties` which consistently fails for me.

Taking a quick peek at the test itself, in `txdbus/test/client_tests.py`...

```
    def test_get_all_properties(self):

        def got_object(ro):
            return ro.callRemote('GetAll', '')

        def got_reply(reply):
            self.assertEquals(reply, {'common'   : 'common1',
                                      'prop1'    : 'foobar',
                                      'prop2'    : 5,
                                      'prop_if1' : 'pif1',
                                      'pwrite'   : 'orig'})

        return self.proxy_chain(got_object, got_reply)
```

...it is calling the `GetAll` method with an empty interface argument. Not being familiar with DBUS specifics, the behavior seems to be returning a "merged" result of two interfaces in `TestClass` at around line 1388: these are `org.txdbus.trial.IFace1` and `org.txdbus.trial.IFace2`.

Given that both interfaces expose/declare a property named `common`, respectively supported by the `common1` and `common2` attributes in the class, it seems that for most cases of the test run, `common1`'s value is returned, but for the particular PYTHONHASHSEED mentioned above, `common2`'s value is returned and the test fails.

Questions on my mind at that point:
1. First things first: what's the specification for the behaviour of `GetAll` when an empty interface argument is passed in? Is it supposed to merge/combine the existing interfaces? If so how?
2. If `GetAll` should return a merged/combined list of all existing interfaces, and assuming that somehow order is important when facing overlapping names, then the current implementation needs fixing.
3. If, otherwise, no such speficification for `GetAll` exists -- and, say, the result may be arbitrary (again, I have no idea) --  then the test may need to be adapted to account for that possibility.

Then I skimmed through https://dbus.freedesktop.org/doc/dbus-specification.html#standard-interfaces-properties which apparently states that:

> An empty string may be provided for the interface name; in this case, if there are multiple properties on an object with the same name, the results are undefined (picking one by according to an arbitrary deterministic rule, or returning an error, are the reasonable possibilities).

So, it seems 3. above is the case -- unless txdbus wants to be stricter in this scenario, of course. :)

issue_title: Method interface, is it not complete, how to put async callback?
issue_body:
Hi

Browsing to the code, it looks like Method interface is not complete. In ur code u have implemented
Method('exampleMethod', arguments='s', returns='s' ) 
So it takes arguments and returns only...
But the original package is 

method(dbus_interface, in_signature=None, out_signature=None, async_callbacks=None, sender_keyword=None, path_keyword=None, destination_keyword=None, message_keyword=None, connection_keyword=None, byte_arrays=False, rel_path_keyword=None, **kwargs)
source code 
Factory for decorators used to mark methods of a dbus.service.Object to be exported on the D-Bus.

The decorated method will be exported over D-Bus as the method of the same name on the given D-Bus interface.

Parameters:
dbus_interface (str) - Name of a D-Bus interface
in_signature (str or None) - If not None, the signature of the method parameters in the usual D-Bus notation
out_signature (str or None) - If not None, the signature of the return value in the usual D-Bus notation
async_callbacks (tuple containing (str,str), or None) - If None (default) the decorated method is expected to return values matching the out_signature as usual, or raise an exception on error. If not None, the following applies:

async_callbacks contains the names of two keyword arguments to the decorated function, which will be used to provide a success callback and an error callback (in that order).

When the decorated method is called via the D-Bus, its normal return value will be ignored; instead, a pair of callbacks are passed as keyword arguments, and the decorated method is expected to arrange for one of them to be called.

On success the success callback must be called, passing the results of this method as positional parameters in the format given by the out_signature.

On error the decorated method may either raise an exception before it returns, or arrange for the error callback to be called with an Exception instance as parameter.

issue_title: train_object_detector example shows incorrect testing results
issue_body:
While training some object detectors I have found that when detector is loaded from .svm file and used in real-world scenario, its precision is different from the results shown at training process. Precision/Recall/AP results are different. And the reason is because my training/testing data has many overlapped objects

I have found that when object detector is trained [in sample code](https://github.com/davisking/dlib/blob/master/examples/train_object_detector.cpp), line  291:
` object_detector<image_scanner_type> detector = trainer.train(images, object_locations, ignore);
`
Trainer has no custom test_box_overlap initialized in its params and it will make automatic test_box_overlap training. When the process will be finished - detector will have some trained test_box_overlap parameters.

But when sample code is testing object detector (line 297) - it does call test_object_detector function with default argument test_box_overlap:

`cout << "Test detector (precision,recall,AP): " << test_object_detection_function(detector, images, object_locations) << endl;`

So test_object_detector will not use trained test_box_overlap instance from detector, but will use some default-constructed one for making decisions about detector's precision. Really - threre will be two different overlap testers used - one when detector makes detections and other for calculating precision. 

Is this the correct way to calculate detector's precision? 

May be we should update sample code to use detector's trained overlap tester when calling test_object_detector? 

`

issue_title: Append changeset hash to version if it's not tagged
issue_body:
Originally reported by: **Anonymous**

----------------------------------------

At the moment, hgtools returns a version like N.N.Ndev when getting the version name of a changeset that isn't tagged. This means that multiple changesets will be given the same version name, making the name less meaningful than it could be.

Git's describe function (by default) produces something like the following when called on a commit without a tag:

<latest-tag>-<commits-since-tag>-g<partial-commit-hash>

e.g., 1.4.3-3-g344b483

Because of this, every commit is almost guaranteed to have a unique version name, which is much more useful. It'd be useful if hgtools provided something similar.

----------------------------------------
- Bitbucket: https://bitbucket.org/jaraco/hgtools/issue/15

issue_title: remove RuntimeError exception, formally NoKeyboard, or change to filter?
issue_body:
Upstream EK proposed a custom NoKeyboard exception, https://github.com/erikrose/blessings/commit/327e31595fd1c79f9bb4c4d209e753cbde1e0c60 which was backmerged in 1af188b50248c794b671e55df5659e5935fdc799.

Downstream, we derive Terminal to serve over telnet, ssh, rlogin, and others. This sort of rejection of parameter combinations appears unpythonic, in that it then requires "patching" to ensure such exception is not raised:

```python
    """ A thin wrapper over :class:`blessed.Terminal`. """

    _session = None

    def __init__(self, kind, stream, rows, columns):
        """ Class initializer. """
        self._rows = rows
        self._columns = columns
        BlessedTerminal.__init__(self, kind, stream)
        # *PATCH* against 'is None' check in method Terminal.inkey()
        # that raises RuntimeError to "prevent indefinite blocking
        # timeout without a keyboard attached" -- which is often our
        # intention.
        self._keyboard_fd = 'defunc'
        if sys.platform.lower().startswith('win32'):
            self._normal = '\x1b[m'
```

issue_title: exceptions raised from parsers should get converted to ConfigurationErrors
issue_body:
Right now, parsers raise all kinds of errors which bubble up to the application code that's trying to pull a configuration key. That means that the exception that gets generated is missing a lot of context and it's not clear that the error is probably a configuration error and what the user can do to figure it out.

Instead of doing that, when a parser throws an exception, the exception should get wrapped in a ConfigurationError with a more helpful error message that includes the key and namespace. Then a user can see that and have a much better idea of what to do.

For example, this is useless:

```
web_1          | ValueError: logging level must be one of "DEBUG", "INFO", "WARNING", "ERROR", "CRITICAL"
```

I have no idea the problem is with the value of the configuration variable titled "LOGGING_LEVEL". (That's technically not true. I wrote Antenna, so I know exactly what the problem is.)

This is better:

```
ConfigurationError: USERNAME (healthcheck) requires a value parseable by <type 'str'>
```

Though we should fix the syntax a bit.

issue_title: Enhancement Request - Provide option to log all read/writes from device to file equivalent to putty session log
issue_body:
Kirk - First thanks for Netmiko, I've gotten a lot mileage out of it already- I was looking through the code and didn't find a way to capture all output and commands applied to the device by Netmiko, beyond just the responses from explicit send_command/config calls. The idea here being equivalent to a putty or securecrt session log that saves everything the device sees and responds with.

I think the use case here would be not only debugging (both netmiko as well as user scripts that make use of netmiko), but would also provide a sort of audit trail if that were desired by the user.

Now that most all I/O is consolidated down to the read/write_channel methods I think this might be feasible, perhaps selecting on the existing `verbose` option or maybe a new `session_log` option. If you think this is worth looking into I can try my hand at a patch.

Thoughts?

issue_title: Use of deprecated method logger.warn
issue_body:
`log.warn` should be changed to `log.warning` to prevent DeprecationWarnings.

I suggest also at the same time switching on `import warnings; warnings.filterwarnings('error')` when running the test suite, so that you'll find deprecation warnings automatically in the future.

issue_title: Handle non-string 'Authorization' header more gracefully
issue_body:
I'm adding mohawk to a legacy web.py project, and was performing the http auth header lookup like so:
```python
auth_header = web.ctx.environ.get('HTTP_AUTHORIZATION')
...
Receiver(lookup_hawk_credentials,
         auth_header,
         url,
         ...)
```

However if the auth header isn't present, this results in a value of `None` being passed to the Receiver constructor, which results in:

```
  File "/home/Ed/.virtualenvs/of/lib/python2.7/site-packages/mohawk/receiver.py", line 82, in __init__
    parsed_header = parse_authorization_header(request_header)
  File "/home/Ed/.virtualenvs/of/lib/python2.7/site-packages/mohawk/util.py", line 158, in parse_authorization_header
    parts = auth_header.split(',')
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'split'
```

Whilst the API docs do state that the auth header should be a string:
http://mohawk.readthedocs.org/en/latest/api.html#receiver

...they say the same for the others that default to `None`, so it's easy to think that a `None`-value will be handled.

Instead perhaps it should raise either a generic `HawkFail` (like happens if the header doesn't start with 'hawk') or else a new exception for missing header?
http://mohawk.readthedocs.org/en/latest/api.html#exceptions

(Using mohawk 0.3.1)

issue_title: Fast, Non-lazy Completions.type
issue_body:
Hey there !

For once not a bug, but a request for improvement. 
With current IPython integration, I need to greedily compute the `.type` of all completions.
For the terminal version I could it lazily, notebook I can't. In some case (`matplotlib.` ) this can be gargantuesqually slow (don't know if it's an english adverb, but anyway), and AFAICT, jedi is reparsing – or not reusing some available context.

I do understand it can be hard to "Guess" if the context have changed.

So is there a way to tell jedi (like using a context manager): Thinks are not going to change underneath you, can you compute all these things at once ?

Right now it seem like an LRU cache in `mixed.py` does the trick ( line 120ish)

```
@functools.lru_cache()
def parse(grammar, path):
    with open(path) as f:
        source = f.read()
    source = common.source_to_unicode(source)
    return FastParser(grammar, source, path)
```

Though I understand that FastParser is _also_ made to recompute only what's necessary using DiffParser. But FastParser is supposed to have CachedFastParser as a metaclass so should it be cached .. but is not... Anyway getting late let me know if things make sens

issue_title: Delorean support for timedelta arithmetic

The timedelta class in the datetime package is very useful for calendar/time arithmetic. It would be great if Deloreans had the same behavior as datetime objects:

```
from datetime import timedelta, datetime
from delorean import Delorean

dd1 = Delorean(datetime(2013, 2, 15), "US/Eastern")
dd2 = Delorean(datetime(2013, 2, 16), "US/Eastern")

one_day = dd2 - dd1
assert one_day == timedelta(days=1)
assert (dd1 + one_day) == dd2
```

issue_title: Allow passing options (-T and -t) to guessit so that movie names with string "season" are correctly parsed
issue_body:
Trying to download subtitles for movie _The.Delinquent.Season.2017.720p.WEBRip.x264-[YTS.AM].mp4_:

```
paulo:~/tmp$ subliminal --debug download -l en The.Delinquent.Season.2017.720p.WEBRip.x264-\[YTS.AM\].mp4
Collecting videos  [####################################]  100%  The.Delinquent.Season.2017.720p.WEBRip.x264-[YTS.AM].mp4DEBUG:subliminal.cli:Collecting path The.Delinquent.Season.2017.720p.WEBRip.x264-[YTS.AM].mp4
INFO:subliminal.core:Scanning video 'The.Delinquent.Season.2017.720p.WEBRip.x264-[YTS.AM].mp4' in ''
ERROR:subliminal.cli:Unexpected error while collecting path The.Delinquent.Season.2017.720p.WEBRip.x264-[YTS.AM].mp4
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/home/paulo/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/subliminal-2.1.0.dev0-py3.6.egg/subliminal/cli.py", line 349, in download
    video = scan_video(p)
  File "/home/paulo/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/subliminal-2.1.0.dev0-py3.6.egg/subliminal/core.py", line 430, in scan_video
    video = Video.fromguess(path, guessit(path))
  File "/home/paulo/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/subliminal-2.1.0.dev0-py3.6.egg/subliminal/video.py", line 93, in fromguess
    return Episode.fromguess(name, guess)
  File "/home/paulo/.local/lib/python3.6/site-packages/subliminal-2.1.0.dev0-py3.6.egg/subliminal/video.py", line 170, in fromguess
    raise ValueError('Insufficient data to process the guess')
ValueError: Insufficient data to process the guess
Collecting videos  [####################################]  100%
0 video collected / 0 video ignored / 1 error
```

I suspected and confirmed that `guessit` might be parsing the movie as en episode because of the string _season_:

```
paulo:~/tmp$ guessit The.Delinquent.Season.2017.720p.WEBRip.x264-\[YTS.AM\].mp4
For: The.Delinquent.Season.2017.720p.WEBRip.x264-[YTS.AM].mp4
GuessIt found: {
    "title": "The Delinquent",
    "season": 2017,
    "year": 2017,
    "screen_size": "720p",
    "source": "Web",
    "other": "Rip",
    "video_codec": "H.264",
    "release_group": "YTS.AM",
    "container": "mp4",
    "mimetype": "video/mp4",
    "type": "episode"
}
```

To make `guessit` parse it correctly, I found out I had to explicitly say it was a movie:

```
paulo:~/tmp$ guessit -t movie The.Delinquent.Season.2017.720p.WEBRip.x264-\[YTS.AM\].mp4
For: The.Delinquent.Season.2017.720p.WEBRip.x264-[YTS.AM].mp4
GuessIt found: {
    "title": "The Delinquent Season",
    "year": 2017,
    "screen_size": "720p",
    "source": "Web",
    "other": "Rip",
    "video_codec": "H.264",
    "release_group": "YTS.AM",
    "container": "mp4",
    "mimetype": "video/mp4",
    "type": "movie"
}
```

But then I couldn't find any `subliminal` option to pass `-t movie` to `guessit`. If it isn't indeed possible, could I ask that as a feature request ?

**PS:** For this particular movie, a workaround was renaming it to _The.Delinquent.Season.720p.WEBRip.x264-[YTS.AM].mp4_: `guessit` then parsed it correctly as a movie, and `subliminal` could go along with that.

LO on OpenBSD uses different path, so here's patch:

diff --git a/unoconv b/unoconv

 extrapaths = glob.glob('/usr/lib*/libreoffice*/basis*/program') + \
+             glob.glob('/usr/local/lib/libreoffice*/basis*/program') + \

              glob.glob('/usr/lib*/openoffice*/basis*/program') + \
```

issue_title: KafkaProducer hangs when publishing to new topic (and using uWSGI master process)
issue_body:
I'm trying to upgrade from `SimpleProducer` to the newer `KafkaProducer`, but I'm running into an issue where the producer hangs when it tries to publish to a topic that doesn't exist. This occurs only when the producer is run with uWSGI, **and** uWSGI is configured to use a master process.

The messages are successfully published if uWSGI is run without a master process or if the topic already exists.

This behavior manifests with both kafka 0.9.0.1 and 0.10.0.

It seems like this might be related to http://stackoverflow.com/questions/37693563/how-to-make-kafka-python-or-pykafka-work-as-an-async-producer-with-uwsgi-and-gev and https://github.com/dpkp/kafka-python/issues/709, but the fact that it only breaks for nonexistent topics makes me think it might be a separate issue.

issue_title: URL not extracted if `.` at end of url
issue_body:
Hi! Thanks for an amazing library. I've successfully used it to extract urls from 2400 documents. But there are just a few, where extraction isn't happening. 

The following content is similar to many other documents that I've extracted from which is why I was surprised it wasn't working.

Here are some example docs:

https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1636023/000117184317003392/0001171843-17-003392.txt
https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1636023/000117184317003392/exh_101.htm
https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1636023/000117184317003392/fsd_053117.htm

Each of the above docs contains the url `www.westrock.com`

I finally figured it out that a period at the end of the url was causing it to not be detected.
I tried futzing with `set_after_tld_chars` and `set_stop_chars` but didn't get anywhere.

I used `content.replace('.com.', '.com')` as a pre-processing workaround (but obviously this isn't particularly scalable as there are lots of other domain extensions)

Thanks again for an amazing library.

(0002, 0000) File Meta Information Group Length
(0002, 0001) File Meta Information Version 
(0002, 0010) Transfer Syntax UID
(0002, 0012) Implementation Class UID 
(0002, 0013) Implementation Version Name   

In filewriter.py _write_file_meta_info I get a ValueError raised with missing tags (0002,0002) and (0002,0003).

I resolved the issue by adding the following (line 255  filewriter.py)

    if Tag((2, 2)) not in meta_dataset:
            meta_dataset.add_new((2, 2), 'UI', "")   # file meta information version
    if Tag((2, 3)) not in meta_dataset:
            meta_dataset.add_new((2, 3), 'UI', "")   # file meta information version

but am not sure if this is the right approach.

issue_title: Add the capability to fetch a linked object directly from the linking object
issue_body:
A suggestion from the [Slack channel](https://unmethours.slack.com/archives/eppy-dev):

>julien: is there a way to directly fetch an object linked? like for a electricloadcenter:distribution, I want to fetch the object from the "Inverter Name" field?

>jamie_bull: you'd like to be able to call `linked_object = myobject.Inverter_Name.something` and have it return the EpBunch that represents the inverter?
>jamie_bull: like `myobject.Inverter_Name.object` maybe?

To do this as it stands you need to know the `key` of the object being referred to:
`idf.getobject(key, myobject.Inverter_Name)`

I think this might be possible after the changes in #102. What we need to do is fetch the key from the `\ref` field in the IDD.

issue_title: -r / --rcfile / rcfile option: Path including space char does not work on Windows
issue_body:
## Problem Description ##

The [MeCab 0.996 option flags](http://mecab.googlecode.com/svn/trunk/mecab/doc/mecab.html) ```-r``` / ```--rcfile``` / ```rcfile``` for specifying a resource file will not work via natto-py when the path is a Windows path including whitespace chars.

## Steps to reproduce the problem ##

1. Install MeCab 0.996.exe on Windows XP
2. Install Python 2.7.8
3. Install natto-py 0.0.2
4. Execute the following:
```
#!python
import natto
import os

os.environ['MECAB_PATH']='C:/Program Files/MeCab/bin/libmecab.dll'
os.environ['MECAB_CHARSET']='shift-jis'

nm = natto.MeCab("-rC:\Program Files\MeCab\etc\mymecabrc")
```

* Observe that the above code results in the following exception being thrown:

```
#!python

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "natto\api.py", line 273, in __init__
    raise MeCabError(self._ERROR_INIT % str(verr))
natto.api.MeCabError: Could not initialize MeCab: unrecognized arguments: -rC:\Program Files\MeCab\etc\mymecabrc
```

## Expected behavior ##

The reference to mecab via natto-py should be successfully instantiated.

Note that using ```-r``` / ```--rcfile``` from the mecab command-line will work by simply surrounding the Windows path with double-quotes.

```
#!script

mecab -r"C:\Program Files\MeCab\etc\mymecabrc"
```

## Version, platform, environment details ##

1. MeCab 0.996.exe
2. Windows XP Professional, Version 2002, Service Pack 3
3. Python 2.7.8
4. natto-py 0.0.2

issue_title: Add support for feature constraint parsing
synthesize() clips notes when they overlap in time and pitch
issue_body:
If the MIDI file contains two notes (in the same instrument) that have the same pitch and overlap in time, the second of the two will not get synthesized. I.e., say I have a C4 from 1-3s, and another C4 from 2.5-4s: the second note gets "killed" during synthesis.

Admittedly two overlapping notes by the same instrument is not physically possible for all instruments (it is for a guitar for example though), but I think that the correct behavior should be to give the onset of the second note priority over the offset of the first note?

issue_title: GetCookieManager sometimes not called due to a race condition
issue_body:
The `GetCookieManager` callback doesn't have `browser` parameter in upstream CEF. This param is set during synchronous browser creation in `CreateBrowserSync` [1], so it is not immediately available. See comment in request context handler in C++ [2] and the SetBrowser method. Fix needs to be applied in [3] with such code:

```py
if cefBrowser.get():
    pyBrowser = GetPyBrowser(cefBrowser, "GetCookieManager")
else:
    pyBrowser = None
```

And app code needs to be aware that `browser` param in GetCookieManager may be None - API docs need to state that.

Issue originally reported on the forum:
https://groups.google.com/d/msg/cefpython/e3bFx1I5BZA/2ZM7jKk8AgAJ

Ref:
[1] https://github.com/cztomczak/cefpython/blob/ebdb18446f399ee0dd6d47c0722ab866ba24d0b0/src/cefpython.pyx#L908
[2] https://github.com/cztomczak/cefpython/blob/824d7ce1efacde0a9f0e453cd69325d23fe3e7a9/src/client_handler/request_context_handler.h#L21
[3] https://github.com/cztomczak/cefpython/blob/77c506b7c337a83aabda51f00df9f6a17c47d75e/src/handlers/request_handler.pyx#L281

issue_title: Parsing Speed 50x slower than regex
issue_body:
I was incorporating pygrok in a project to parse blacklist ips. It is only about 0.5MB in total, only 2 or 3 files. It took 70 seconds to parse through each row in the files. I didn't realize it was due to the pygrok, so I did multi-threading on it. And the speed is still considerably slow. The files are on HDD, not SSD, but then again, 0.5 MB file, it shouldn't take that long no matter SSD or HDD. 

And now I replaced pygrok with my regex pattern and it loaded the records, parsed them, within 0.04 seconds. Why is the speed so different? I understand grok patterns are also running on regular expression patterns in the lower level. Is there anything I can do to help investigate this?

issue_body:
I wanted to discuss this feature PR before actually taking any action.

One application I wanted was to be able to take the generated QR code and display it on an HTML page, without writing anything to disk.

As it turns out, this is possible:

```python
# mytext was created
qrcode = pq.create(mytext)
buffer = BytesIO()
qrcode.png(buffer, scale=5)
enc = b64encode(buffer.getvalue()).decode('utf-8')
```

I was wondering if you might be interested in accepting a PR that defines the following API?

```python
encoding = qrcode.base64(scale=5)
```

Please let me know, I'm happy to work on the PR for this.

issue_title: Benchmarks for rollouts and training
Problem with euler conventions ?
issue_body:
Dear, 

I were using the simplest code because my results were not correct.

The convention of the different object seems to be different

* [Matrix33 Euler convention](https://github.com/adamlwgriffiths/Pyrr/blob/master/pyrr/matrix33.py#L45-L47) is [pitch, roll, yaw]
* [Matrix44 Euler convention](https://github.com/adamlwgriffiths/Pyrr/blob/master/pyrr/matrix44.py#L62) is like Matrix33 : [pitch, roll, yaw]
* [Euler object convention](https://github.com/adamlwgriffiths/Pyrr/blob/master/pyrr/euler.py) : [pitch, roll, yaw]
* [Quaternion Euler convention](https://github.com/adamlwgriffiths/Pyrr/blob/master/pyrr/quaternion.py#L120-L130) is [yaw, pitch, roll]



Exemple : 
```
import numpy as np
from pyrr import *

roll = 60
pitch = 0
yaw = 0

def deg2rad(arr): 
	for i in xrange(len(arr)):
		arr[i] = 2*float(arr[i])*np.pi/180
	return arr

Eulers = [yaw,pitch,roll]
Eulers = deg2rad(Eulers)

matrix33 = Matrix33.from_eulers(Eulers)
q = Quaternion.from_eulers(Eulers)
print "Matrix33 :" 
print matrix33
print "Quaternion :"
print q.matrix33
```
Result : 
```
Matrix33 :
[[ 0.5        0.         0.8660254]
 [ 0.         1.        -0.       ]
 [-0.8660254  0.         0.5      ]]
Quaternion :
[[-0.5        0.8660254  0.       ]
 [-0.8660254 -0.5        0.       ]
 [ 0.        -0.         1.       ]]
```

Issue with "using" type definition
issue_body:
Breathe seems unable to deal with certain usages of the ``using`` keyword as a type-definition directive. A self-contained example is available here:

https://github.com/bluescarni/breathe_bug_01

(first run doxygen from the ``doxygen`` subdir, then ``make html`` from the ``sphinx`` subdir)

There is a class template of this form:

```c++
template <int N = 0>
class mp_integer;
```

And a type definition:

```c++
using integer = mp_integer<>;
```

The warnings raised by sphinx:

```console
sphinx-build -b html -d _build/doctrees   . _build/html
Running Sphinx v1.4.4
making output directory...
loading pickled environment... not yet created
building [mo]: targets for 0 po files that are out of date
building [html]: targets for 1 source files that are out of date
updating environment: 1 added, 0 changed, 0 removed
reading sources... [100%] index                                                                                                                               
/home/bluescarni/repos/breathe_bug_01/sphinx/index.rst:12: WARNING: Error in type declaration.
If typedef-like declaration:
  Type must be either just a name or a typedef-like declaration.
  If just a name:
    Error in declarator or parameters and qualifiers
    Invalid definition: Expected identifier in nested name, got keyword: using [error at 5]
      using piranha::integer = typedef mp_integer<>
      -----^
  If typedef-like declaration:
    Invalid definition: Expected identifier in nested name, got keyword: using [error at 5]
      using piranha::integer = typedef mp_integer<>
      -----^
If type alias or template alias:

If ``using`` is replaced with a ``typedef``, the warnings go away and the html output is displayed as expected.

issue_title: Record mode 'new_episodes' broken
issue_body:
I noticed that record mode `new_episodes` doesn't work as it used to and as I expect it to work. It records new interaction even if absolutely identical one already exists in the current cassette. Please take a look at following code:
```
import time
import pytest
from betamax import Betamax
from requests import Session


def test_new_episodes():
    url = 'https://github.com/sigmavirus24/betamax/'
    session = Session()
    cassette_name = 'test_new_episodes_{}'.format(time.time())
    recorder = Betamax(session, default_cassette_options={'record_mode': 'new_episodes'})
    with recorder.use_cassette(cassette_name):
        session.get(url)
    recorder = Betamax(session, default_cassette_options={'record_mode': 'new_episodes'})
    with recorder.use_cassette(cassette_name):
        session.get(url)
        assert len(recorder.current_cassette.interactions) == 1
```
This test passes with version 0.5.1 but fails with all the newer ones.
I think because of [this line](https://github.com/sigmavirus24/betamax/blob/0.8.0/betamax/cassette/cassette.py#L122) matching interaction is never found and new one gets recorded.

issue_title: Rendezvous should not send list of in-use nameplates to clients
issue_body:
In a high volume relay, an attacker looking to intercept files can use the `_list` function to find nameplates waiting for a receiver, and use them to attempt file interception. On a high traffic relay, say 2-3 transfers a second, you have about a 30% chance of an intercept over the course of 2 hours. 

If we prevent auto-complete on nameplates however, an attacker would have to guess nameplates to find one with a sender listening. Moreover, if they are rapidly polling low nameplates, legitimate users would be less likely to receive those nameplates from the server, especially if the rate limiting is implemented on lonely nameplates.

issue_title: Django create_user fails if USERNAME_FIELD is not set to default 'username'
issue_body:
I have a problem of python-social-auth failing on Django if [USERNAME_FIELD](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.8/topics/auth/customizing/#django.contrib.auth.models.CustomUser) is set to anything other than `'username'`, i.e. `'email'`. 

I have debugged to understand that the cause of the problem is due to `create_user()` in `django_orm.py` [popping out](https://github.com/omab/python-social-auth/blob/master/social/storage/django_orm.py#L59) `username` kwarg and replacing it with, in my case, `email` kwarg before calling the model manager's [create_user](https://docs.djangoproject.com/en/1.8/ref/contrib/auth/#django.contrib.auth.models.UserManager.create_user) method.

To me that is completely unexpectant, as I don't understand why this is done, and why `USERNAME_FiELD` is taken into account on user creation, when it's supposed to be used only by authentication backends.

issue_title: Deletion of nested inlines doesn't go through
issue_body:
If you have models:
```python
class Parent:
    ...
class Child:
     parent = fk(Parent)
class Grandchild:
    parent = fk(Child)
```
and you create these reversions:
```python
with reversion.create_revision():
    parent = Parent.objects.create()
    child_a = Child.objects.create(
        parent=parent)
    grandchild_a = Grandchild.objects.create(
        parent=child_a)

with reversion.create_revision():
    child_b = Child.objects.create(
        parent=parent)
    grandchild_b = Grandchild.objects.create(
        parent=child_b)
    reversion.add_to_revision(parent)
```
and if you then call
```python
 Version.objects.get_for_object(parent)[1].revision.revert(delete=True)
```
you will get an exception in revert function on line:
```python
collector.collect([item for item in current_revision if item not in old_revision])
```
because this list will contain instances of different models, in this case [child_b, grandchild_b]. It doesn't seem like .collect can handle different types, so i suggest calling .collect per each type.

i opened a PR #718

issue_title: Database Helpers broken for SQLite
issue_body:
The Database Helpers, `create_database`, `drop_database`, and `database_exists` do not properly handle SQLite database URL based on the SQLAlchemy documentation of supported formats.

You should be able to use a URL of "sqlite://" and get an in-memory database, the help functions don't handle this use case properly.

There is also a bug with `drop_database` that just incorrectly uses the wrong `url.database` reference instead of `database` like the rest of the database types.

issue_title: Index not properly recreated on `Null` to `Not Null` migration
issue_body:
When running migrations in Django 1.8, if a field is altered from being nullable to not allowing nulls, any indexes for it are not recreated or dropped, which causes an error. The exact error should look like below (with field names substituted):

```
django.db.utils.ProgrammingError: ('42000', "[42000] [Microsoft][ODBC SQL Server Driver][SQL Server]The index '<INDEX_NAME>' is dependent on column '<COLUMN_NAME>'. (5074) (SQLExecDirectW)")
```

To work around this issue, we had to drop the index manually (using alter field and setitng `db_index` to `False`), alter the field to no longer be nullable, then alter the indexing back to true to create the index. This should, however, be handled by default.

---

**Old Migration:**

``` python
migrations.AlterField(
    model_name='<MODEL_NAME>',
    name='<FIELD_NAME>',
    field=models.ForeignKey(related_name='+', to='<RELATION_NAME>', null=False),
),
```

---

**New Migration:**

``` python
migrations.AlterField(
    model_name='<MODEL_NAME>',
    name='<FIELD_NAME>',
    field=models.ForeignKey(related_name='+', to='<RELATION_NAME>', null=True, db_index=False),
),
migrations.AlterField(
    model_name='<MODEL_NAME>',
    name='<FIELD_NAME>',
    field=models.ForeignKey(related_name='+', to='<RELATION_NAME>', null=False),
),
migrations.AlterField(
    model_name='<MODEL_NAME>',
    name='<FIELD_NAME>',
    field=models.ForeignKey(related_name='+', to='<RELATION_NAME>', null=False, db_index=True),
),
```

issue_title: RecursionError in some sort of obscure IPython/prettyprinter interaction case
issue_body:
* PrettyPrinter version: 0.4.0
* Python version: 3.6
* Operating System: Linux

After configuring IPython to use `prettyprinter` by putting this in my IPython startup file:

<details>

```python
try:
    import prettyprinter
except ImportError:
    pass
else:
    from prettyprinter.color import GitHubLightStyle
    ipy = get_ipython()
    ipy.colors = 'LightBG'
    ipy.highlighting_style = GitHubLightStyle

    prettyprinter.install_extras(
        # Comment out any packages you are not using.
        include=[
            'ipython',
            'attrs',
            #'django',
        ],
        warn_on_error=True
    )
```
</details>

I was then curious how `prettyprinter` would work with code that actually tries to take advantage of IPython's smart pretty printer. (To reproduce, I think an environment with `pip install ipython patsy prettyprinter` should be sufficient.) The result was, well... not pretty:

```
In [1]: import patsy

In [2]: patsy.parse_formula.parse_formula("y ~ x1 + x2")

--> 803         return dispatch(args[0].__class__)(*args, **kw)
    804 
    805     registry[object] = func

~/.user-python3.6-64bit/lib/python3.6/site-packages/prettyprinter/prettyprinter.py in _run_pretty(pretty_fn, value, ctx, trailing_comment)

--> 359     return repr(value)

~/.user-python3.6-64bit/lib/python3.6/site-packages/patsy/util.py in repr_pretty_delegate(obj)
    420     if optional_dep_ok and "IPython" in sys.modules:

--> 422         return pretty(obj)

~/.user-python3.6-64bit/lib/python3.6/site-packages/IPython/lib/pretty.py in pretty(obj, verbose, max_width, newline, max_seq_length)
    138     stream = StringIO()

--> 140     printer.pretty(obj)

... last 9 frames repeated, from the frame below ...

~/.user-python3.6-64bit/lib/python3.6/site-packages/prettyprinter/extras/ipython.py in pretty(self, obj)

---> 27             cpprint(obj, stream=self.stream, style=style, width=columns, end=None)
     28

RecursionError: maximum recursion depth exceeded while calling a Python object
```

This is trying to pretty-print a [`patsy.infix_parser.ParseNode`](https://github.com/pydata/patsy/blob/924b387652d13f816fd4a32983aae2ccba38fd84/patsy/infix_parser.py#L75), whose [`__repr__`](https://github.com/pydata/patsy/blob/924b387652d13f816fd4a32983aae2ccba38fd84/patsy/infix_parser.py#L82) uses a helper [`repr_pretty_delegate`](https://github.com/pydata/patsy/blob/924b387652d13f816fd4a32983aae2ccba38fd84/patsy/util.py#L432-L433) which just calls `IPython.lib.pretty.pretty`, because I didn't want to reimplement `__repr__` twice, once normally and once using IPython's system. I guess the problem is that you're monkeypatching `IPython.lib.pretty.RepresentationPrinter.pretty`, so we end up in a loop where you don't recognize my object so you try to call `__repr__`, then my `__repr__` tries to call IPython's `pretty`, which goes back into your code and doesn't recognize my object so you try to call `__repr__`...

(BTW, you may also appreciate [`repr_pretty_impl`](https://github.com/pydata/patsy/blob/924b387652d13f816fd4a32983aae2ccba38fd84/patsy/util.py#L437-L454), which is exactly the same interface as your `pretty_call`, but implemented using IPython's pretty-printer and about 5 years ago. And in case you're curious, here's what IPython prints for that code if you don't have prettyprinter enabled:
<details>

```
In [2]: patsy.parse_formula.parse_formula("y ~ x1 + x2")
Out[2]: 
ParseNode('~',
          Token('~', <Origin y ->~<- x1 + x2 (2-3)>),
          [ParseNode('PYTHON_EXPR',
                     Token('PYTHON_EXPR',
                           <Origin ->y<- ~ x1 + x2 (0-1)>,
                           extra='y'),
                     []),
           ParseNode('+',
                     Token('+', <Origin y ~ x1 ->+<- x2 (7-8)>),
                     [ParseNode('PYTHON_EXPR',
                                Token('PYTHON_EXPR',
                                      <Origin y ~ ->x1<- + x2 (4-6)>,
                                      extra='x1'),
                                []),
                      ParseNode('PYTHON_EXPR',
                                Token('PYTHON_EXPR',
                                      <Origin y ~ x1 + ->x2<- (9-11)>,
                                      extra='x2'),
                                [])])])
```
</details>
So you can see why I'm interested in this... :-))

Any ideas on how to avoid this? I suppose one obvious solution would be to go through and register `prettyprinter` printers for all my objects. It would be nice if `prettyprinter` could do something more out-of-the-box compatible here, though, so to improve the possibilities of it becoming a standard thing in IPython -- maybe if an object has a `_repr_pretty_` it could dispatch back into the real un-monkeypatched IPython formatting system?

Also, is there any way to do something like this `__repr__` → `prettyprinter` trick to avoid implementing all my reprs multiple times?

issue_title: request from aiohttp
issue_body:
Below I have two implementations of passing the aiohttp request to the AsyncNamespace methods. Are these methods fragile? Is there a better place upstream where I should be doing this? (i.e. will this work across all Managers?)

A wrapper that passes the aiohttp request.

```
from collections import OrderedDict
import functools
import inspect

import socketio


def pass_request(func):
    argspec = inspect.getargspec(func).args

    @functools.wraps(func)
    async def wrapper(namespace, *args):
        arg_map = OrderedDict(zip(argspec, (namespace, *args, None)))
        request = namespace.server.environ[arg_map['sid']]['aiohttp.request']
        arg_map['request'] = request
        rv = func(*arg_map.values())
        return await rv if inspect.isawaitable(rv) else rv
    return wrapper


class SocketNamesapce(socketio.AsyncNamespace):
    @pass_request
    def on_connect(self, sid, environ, request):
        # pylint: disable=no-self-use
        # pylint: disable=unused-argument
        print("connect ", sid)

    def on_disconnect(self, sid):
        # pylint: disable=no-self-use
        print('disconnect ', sid)

    @pass_request
    async def on_message(self, sid, data, request):
        print("message ", data)
        await self.emit('message', data, room=sid)
```

A subclass for AsyncNamespace that provide the request object if the method is requesting it.
```
class SocketNamesapce(socketio.AsyncNamespace):
    async def trigger_event(self, event, *args):
        handler_name = 'on_' + event
        callback = getattr(self, handler_name, None)
        if callback:
            argspec = inspect.getargspec(callback).args
            if 'request' in argspec:
                args = (*args, self.server.environ[args[0]]['aiohttp.request'])
        await super().trigger_event(event, *args)

    def on_connect(self, sid, environ, request):
        # pylint: disable=no-self-use
        # pylint: disable=unused-argument
        print('request', request)
        print("connect ", sid)

    def on_disconnect(self, sid):
        # pylint: disable=no-self-use
        print('disconnect ', sid)

    async def on_message(self, sid, data, request):
        print('request', request)
        print("message ", data)
        await self.emit('message', data, room=sid)
```

issue_title: Add setting for cache timeout for initial values
issue_body:
Initial values from a template tag are pushed to the app through the Django cache.
The timeout for these values should be controlled through a setting.

issue_title: All travis tests are run against python 3.5
issue_body: See https://docs.travis-ci.com/user/languages/python for setting up multiple versions.

issue_title: German localization files
issue_body: The *.po file for de is marked as fuzzy. So If I use de locale I get english error messages etc.

issue_title: AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'write'
issue_body:
This logger keeps setting `self.stream = None` causing the next logging statement to fail: ``` Setting self.stream = None: <_io.TextIOWrapper name='F:\\SublimeText\\debug2.txt' mode='a' encoding='cp1252'> Setting self.stream = None: <_io.TextIOWrapper name='F:\\SublimeText\\debug2.txt' mode='a' encoding='cp1252'> Setting self.stream = None: <_io.TextIOWrapper name='F:\\SublimeText\\debug2.txt' mode='a' encoding='cp1252'> self.stream on StreamHandler: None, sys.stderr: <_io.TextIOWrapper name='' mode='w' encoding='cp1252'> --- Logging error --- Traceback (most recent call last): File "F:\Python\lib\logging\__init__.py", line 995, in emit stream.write(msg) AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'write' ``` The file is already 250% above the maximum rotation of 50MB, but cannot be rolled because it is opened on the log viewer. There is not problem in not rotating because of that. It can keep growing until I close the viewer. However, what must to set the stream logger to `None` and make the logging fail randomly. I added these lines for debugging: ```python File: Data/Packages/ConcurrentLogHandler/all/concurrent_log_handler/__init__.py 234: 235: def _close(self): 236: """ Close file stream. Unlike close(), we don't tear anything down, we 237: expect the log to be re-opened after rotation.""" 238: if self.stream: 239: try: 240: if not self.stream.closed: 241: # Flushing probably isn't technically necessary, but it feels right 242: self.stream.flush() 243: self.stream.close() 244: finally: 245: sys.stderr.write( "Setting self.stream = None: %s\n" % self.stream ) 246: self.stream = None File: F:/Python/Lib/logging/__init__.py 979: 980: def emit(self, record): 981: """ 982: Emit a record. 983: 984: If a formatter is specified, it is used to format the record. 985: The record is then written to the stream with a trailing newline. If 986: exception information is present, it is formatted using 987: traceback.print_exception and appended to the stream. If the stream 988: has an 'encoding' attribute, it is used to determine how to do the 989: output to the stream. 990: """ 991: try: 992: msg = self.format(record) 993: sys.stderr.write( "self.stream on StreamHandler: %s\n" % self.stream ) 994: stream = self.stream 995: stream.write(msg) 996: stream.write(self.terminator) ``` Full log:

1. Evaluate whether the test is flaky without fixed seed. If not, remove seed. Else move to 2
2. If test is flaky, determine whether it's an actual uncaught edge case. If so, fix the operator. Else move to 3
3. If numerical instability is inevitable, adjust tolerance level appropriately.

issue_title: Unicode: send_multipart/recv_multipart and ZMQStream
issue_body:
According to https://zeromq.github.io/pyzmq/unicode.html#what-this-means-for-pyzmq:
> Still, we recognize the fact that users will quite frequently have unicode strings that they want to send, so we have added socket.<method>_string() wrappers.

However, there is no corresponding syntactic sugar for the send_multipart / recv_multipart socket methods, nor is there a mechanism for auto handling encoding for ZMQStream. In the former case, it would be nice to mimic the send/recv handling and provide send_multipart_string / recv_multipart_string wrappers. For the latter, it might be easier to provide an encoding argument to on_recv/on_recv_stream that defaults to `None`. If it's not `None`, automatically decode each field with the given character set before passing it to the handler.

Without these, things get annoying very quickly on Python 3.

issue_title: PUB/SUB + pyobj needs documentation
issue_body:
I've been trying to transmit python objects using PUB/SUB and `[send/recv]_pyobj`. It turns out that this doesn't work out of the box, due to the way topics work with PUB/SUB. The subscriber needs to set a topic:

    socket.setsockopt_string(zmq.SUBSCRIBE, "")

and the publisher needs to send the topic as the first part of the message

    socket.send_string("", zmq.SNDMORE)
    socket.send_pyobj(data)

The subscriber will have to eat the topic with an extra receive:

    topic = socket.recv_string()
    data = socket.recv_pyobj()

It would be nice if this were better documented - both how to send topics with `send_pyobj`, and that the topic is absolutely necessary in the first place (if the topic isn't set on the subscriber side, no messages go through).

issue_title: Initializing the metadata of a trait
issue_body:
This issue continues discussion of the metadata point from #48.  We'd like to separate the metadata from the constructor arguments in a TraitType's constructor:  Having the two semantically very different kwarg sets interleaved makes it very difficult to understand the constructor and predict what is going to happen.  

```python
x = Int(allow_none=True, sync=True)           # deprecated

# option A1 - pass metadata in as a separate dict
x = Int(allow_none=True, metadata={'sync': True})

# option A2 - shorter argument name to save typing and line length
x = Int(allow_none=True, md={'sync': True})

# option B1 - set metadata in a function chained onto the constructor.  .metadata() should return self
x = Int(allow_none=True).metadata(sync=True)

# option B2 - shorter name
x = Int(allow_none=True).md(sync=True)

# option B3 - short name from atom, which is a good verb for this
x = Int(allow_none=True).tag(sync=True)
```

The tasks defined in the `proj/proj/celery.py` file directly are registering successfully.

However, shared tasks defined in the custom django apps are not registering to the database and not showing up in admin at `/admin/django_celery_beat/periodictask/`.

**proj/proj/celery.py**

```
# .. imports.. #
os.environ.setdefault('DJANGO_SETTINGS_MODULE', 'proj.settings')

celery_app = Celery('proj')
celery_app.config_from_object('django.conf:settings', namespace='CELERY')

```

**proj/app/tasks.py**
```
from celery import shared_task

@shared_task
def app_hello():

```

issue_title: Format issue in list of comparison operators in DDB Docs
issue_body:
I think there's some formatting issue with the documentation which leads to odd/mistaken information when describing the comparison operators available in condition expressions for DynamoDB.

For instance: [readthedocs\Services\DynamoDB\Client\Query](https://boto3.readthedocs.org/en/latest/reference/services/dynamodb.html#DynamoDB.Client.query), and specifically in Request Syntax > Parameters > KeyConditionExpression you can see

> Valid comparisons for the sort key condition are as follows:
> sortKeyName = :sortkeyval - true if the sort key value is equal to :sortkeyval .
> sortKeyName ** :sortkeyval - true if the sort key value is less than :sortkeyval .
> sortKeyName = :sortkeyval - true if the sort key value is less than or equal to :sortkeyval .
> sortKeyName ** :sortkeyval - true if the sort key value is greater than :sortkeyval .
> [...]

I presume the operators in this list are meant to match the listing given by the [AWS DynamoDB DevGuide on Query And Scan](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/amazondynamodb/latest/developerguide/QueryAndScan.html#QueryAndScan.Query), so I suspect some sort of replacement/escaping gone awry somewhere in the pipeline.

_(btw, I did inspect the page source, so these don't seem to be browser-induced char-replacements due to a missing font or anything of that nature)_

issue_title: aws ec2 describe-spot-price-history always raises PaginationError without --max-items param
issue_body:
Hello. First of all, thanks for all your efforts.

I'm using this library through [aws-cli](https://github.com/aws/aws-cli) on **OS X 10.10.3**, **python 3.4.2** with **awscli 1.7.25** and **botocore 0.106.0**.

I tried aws ec2 describe-spot-price-history api to get spot price history but for any regions/AZs, my command raises PaginationError without *--max-items* param.

Here is my command(I typed **us-east-1a** in AZ, **m3.medium** in type):
```
>>> aws ec2 describe-spot-price-history --start-time 2015-05-08T00:00:00 --end-time 2015-05-08T01:00:00 --availability-zone [any AZ] --instance-types [any type(s) the AZ supports]
```

and this is error message:
```
>>> botocore.exceptions.PaginationError: Error during pagination: The same next token was received twice: ['']
```

When I add `--max-items 1` param, then results are successfully returned with NextToken. 

I found that message is created at paginate.py.

I think amazon returns empty string('') to NextToken when there are no more results to return.

According to [amazon document](http://docs.aws.amazon.com/cli/latest/reference/ec2/describe-spot-price-history.html), Next token will be `null` when there are no more results to return but I can't sure that `null` means `None`. That `null` can be empty string? I don't know...

I edited some codes in paginate.py; insert following codes after break at L#111:
```
elif next_token == ['']:
                yield response
                break
```

after that, I could get right results with same command without *--max-items* param.

I'm not sure about more usage of same function(class) so I can't say my work is right, but NextToken with empty string should be handled. In current version, previous_next_token only can handle None.

Please review this issue. Thanks.

issue_title: Issues with dexterity collections and PTG
issue_body:
I noticed some problems with dexterity collections from plone.app.contenttypes in Plone4.3 and Plone5. Namely they do not work anymore.
There are four problems:
1) The collections adapter is bound to plone.app.collections.interfaces.ICollection but dexterity collections have plone.app.contenttypes.interfaces.ICollection as marker interface. So the adapter does not fit the interface.
2) In dexterity the collections do no longer expose the getRawQuery function.
See https://community.plone.org/t/where-is-defined-collection-getrawquery-and-how/1040 .
This leads to a stack trace.
3) If this problem is overcome the wrong image type is used in the query.
4) And last but not least. The current code does not work with collections collecting subgalleries.

issue_title: SM Wipe Device API Issue
issue_body:
Upfront Context Disclosure: I lack context on how to determine if the API changed or if this "wrapper" has it wrong, so full disclosure there. As far as I understood, all API endpoints were to be backwards compatible, but maybe the API we have access to isn't the same as the version this library targets.

It seems there's a bit of a mismatch around the API for wiping devices and the function enclosed in this wrapper to invoke that endpoint. The specific endpoint is documented here: https://api.meraki.com/api_docs#wipe-a-device

Looking at the url, it targets a specific device. Comparing to the python variant, it tries to invoke a plural variant (devices) which takes lists of params rather than a single param (eg. 'ids' rather than 'id'). This is a fairly easy change, but again, I cannot tell if it _supposed_ to be plural or not.

issue_title: Problem with cmsplugin-filer-image in latest installer
issue_body:
Hi,

I set up a fresh install of DjangoCMS using this link as the guide: http://docs.django-cms.org/en/develop/introduction/install.html
I chose cms version 3.1, and django version 1.8. The install went fine and runserver brought up the standard page as expected. However, when I edited the page and added an image the plugin instance on the Structure page showed Image <Empty>
I have another cms instance where the images do work. I checked the versions of the installed components and they were slightly earlier versions. The versions that work are:
cmsplugin-filer==1.0.0
django-filer==1.0.2
Pillow==3.0.0

On the latest install that produces the error the versions are:
cmsplugin-filer==1.0.1
django-filer==1.0.6
Pillow==3.1.0

I downgraded these 3 components and I could then add images onto the page.
I thought I should let you know of this error on these versions.

Kind regards
max

issue_title: Installing Pipfile.lock environment dependencies and package seperately
issue_body:
Hi there!  

I've been working with pipenv since December and tracking its prodigious development.  After a lot or reading, "application deployment" is the area of Pipenv/Pipfiles that is still most hazy to me.  This could be a documentation/workflow discussion as much as a code one.

## Primary question
Can the Pipfile.lock be installed seperately from the Python package it's made for?

## Context
I am part of an organization that builds lots of Python apps.  Even though these are "apps" (think [concrete dependencies](https://caremad.io/posts/2013/07/setup-vs-requirement/)), we leverage setup.py as much as possible for tracking metadata, dependencies, and generally trying to be good Python citizens.  Our Pipfiles use the `pipenv install -e .` method to lock the setup.py dependencies into the Pipfile.lock (which then also fixes the editable local package inside the lock).  When it comes time for deployment, the package artifact is zipped up and and sent out to servers for deployment.  On the destination server, the package is unzipped and we run `pipenv install --deploy --system` to set up in our clean environment.

If the Pipfile.lock could be distributed and installed seperately:
* If using source distributions (which aren't kosher for libraries, though it seems they're still common for apps), no need to unpack the source before installation (as in the above Context).
* Python apps could be pre-built as a wheel for distribution.
* Containers builds could keep the relatively unchanging dependency installation step and often-changing package installation steps seperate (don't break the cache) for smoother builds.
* App distributions could be installed and managed on the system generally, rather than as an editable-style local directory.  Or apps and dependencies could be purely virtual (which could have some interesting spin-offs for creating hot-loading tools.)

It's possible that this may be asking pipenv do something it wasn't designed for and that this would be better done in pip (when pip eventually supports Pipfiles).  My research hasn't led me to how Cargo or Ruby have solved this, can anyone multilingually-versed comment?

Whatever the discussion result, I'm happy and able to contribute back to code or documentation.

issue_title: bdist_wheel being run instead of install, advice needed
issue_body:
Hey so in my setup.py https://github.com/AndrewAnnex/SpiceyPy/blob/master/setup.py, I implement the install command for my package as I need to build and bundle a shared library which I do at "build time" aka the run time of my setup.py.

I noticed that on my mac if I run pip install spiceypy to install from pypi,  pip attempts to build a wheel instead of running my install command (I see "Running setup.py bdist_wheel for spiceypy" in the terminal). Because this side-steps the Install command it does hit my code which builds the shared library. If I instead install using --no-cache-dir that appears to trigger the correct behavior (the Install command is executed instead). 

I can see the logic in pip at https://github.com/pypa/pip/blob/b9feb191a90e6ed4ef376d41ea9c1a896861f65b/src/pip/_internal/commands/install.py#L282 which explains why disabling the cache works.

Could I get a recommendation for a fix? It seems that I need to override the behavior of bdist_wheel using the cmdclass dict, but I think that would mean needing to depend on pip and or wheel directly which seems like a bad way to fix. Is there a cleaner way to do this?


Thanks

    dist = pkg_resources.Distribution.from_location(path_item, dist_name, metadata)

if __name__ == "__main__":

issue_title: Don't sign activate.ps1
issue_body:
The PowerShell activation script is signed. This allows users set the PowerShell execution policy to AllSigned. On the other hand, it prevents the script from setting a user-specified prompt, requiring users to manually change the script instead of using the --prompt flag.

Unsigned scripts require the 'unrestricted' execution policy. I don't know any developer that hasn't set the execution policy to 'unrestricted' anyway, as it prevents the ability to run your own scripts.

So I suggest not signing the script, and supporting custom prompts.

issue_title: Producer deadlocks using `block_on_queue_full` with connection loss
issue_body:
**PyKafka version**: 2.2.1
**Kafka version**: 0.9.0.0

If the brokers become unreachable, the producer fails to send the batch. Upon failure, the producer attempts to enqueue the messages again, but if the queue is full and the `block_on_queue_full` producer property is set `True`, then the producer thread becomes deadlocked. Restoring access to the broker doesn't resume the producer.

Steps to reproduce:
1. Run sample given below
2. Set a breakpoint on this line: https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/master/pykafka/producer.py#L440
3. Stop the broker
4. Step into the producer until it ultimately deadlocks on this line: https://github.com/Parsely/pykafka/blob/master/pykafka/producer.py#L524

```
import time
from pykafka import KafkaClient

client = KafkaClient(hosts='vagrant-ist-01:9092')
topic = client.topics['ether']

producer = topic.get_producer(**{'max_queued_messages': 10,
                      'min_queued_messages': 5,
                      'linger_ms': 500,
                      'block_on_queue_full': True})

for i in range(0, 100000):
    print str(i)
    producer.produce(str(i))
    time.sleep(0.1)

producer.clean_up()
```

Also, I noticed `cluster.update()` retries `_max_retries` but doesn't have a back-off implemented, which might be an issue of it's own.

issue_title: Tests fail with Astropy LTS
issue_body:
Using the astropy lts release to run tests fails due to the `test` command failing during the `setup.py` call. This seems to be because the lts version does not contain a module named `command`

```python
>>> import astropy
>>> astropy.__version__
u'1.0.13'
>>> from astropy.tests.command import AstropyTest
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
ImportError: No module named command
```

which means that the `test` command fails due an import error [here](https://github.com/astropy/astropy-helpers/blob/master/astropy_helpers/commands/test.py#L20).

```bash
$ $MAIN_CMD $SETUP_CMD
Freezing version number to ./mosviz/version.py
usage: setup.py [global_opts] cmd1 [cmd1_opts] [cmd2 [cmd2_opts] ...]
   or: setup.py --help [cmd1 cmd2 ...]
   or: setup.py --help-commands
   or: setup.py cmd --help
error: The 'test' command requires the astropy package to be installed and importable.
```

The doc string suggests the command *should* fall back to some backwards compatible implementation, but it seems that whatever that was was [removed](https://github.com/astropy/astropy-helpers/commit/1e7a4e6a699af6c7edbadd9fa867d5e909cbc4e1) as per #307.

Currently this prevents travis builds from completing successfully.

issue_title: Stripe api_version not customizable for stripe.Customer.list
issue_body:
When calling `stripe.Customer.list(api_key=key, api_version='2015-10-16')` with a custom api_version, we get the following error:

```
InvalidRequestError: Request req_I25zAXmrYWc3Mj: Received unknown parameter: api_version
```

This is using python 2.7 via the CLI.

It appears that the `api_version` param is not correctly passed into the `APIRequestor` constructor at https://github.com/stripe/stripe-python/blob/master/stripe/resource.py#L451 . I think it should look like https://github.com/stripe/stripe-python/blob/master/stripe/resource.py#L924 . There might be other places where this happens as well.

We are able to work around this by just setting the global stripe api version using `stripe.api_version='2015-10-16'`.

Thank you!

issue_title: global: SQLite transactions are not properly created
issue_body:
**Scenario:**
- use SQLite
- run something like this:
```python
class Demo(db.Model):
    __tablename__ = 'demo'
    pk = db.Column(sa.Integer, primary_key=True)

... create the application and the tables ...

with app.app_context():
    with db.session.begin_nested():
        d1 = Demo()
        d1.pk = 1
        db.session.add(d1)
    db.session.rollback()
with app.app_context():
    res = Demo.query.all()
    print res
```
You will see that the result is read even though the transaction has been rollbacked.

**Reason:**
We prevented SQLAlchemy from handling transaction scope automatically by changing the `isolation_level` as described here http://docs.sqlalchemy.org/en/latest/dialects/sqlite.html#serializable-isolation-savepoints-transactional-ddl
However we did not send our own `BEGIN` request.
Because of this, the savepoints are really committed.

issue_title: last_login does not get updated
issue_body:
There is a field in the user model of the django.contrib.auth called last_login. This gets updated by the update_last_login function of which I think is fired upon login to the admin. 

neither 
rest_framework_jwt.views.obtain_jwt_token
or
rest_framework_jwt.views.refresh_jwt_token
updates this field in the user model.

Is this by design?

issue_title: Fails to package app on OSX Sierra 10.12.4 (hdiutil: attach failed - image not recognized keka)

Appears to to be a few issues happening here.

1) Fails to find local install of the `kivy.app` so downloads it
2) Fails to download `keka` as part of the installation of `kivy`

issue_title: clip2.py no longer compiles
issue_body:
This test I think used to compile, but I just rebuilt pythran with latest git, and now I get compile
errors:

```python
import numpy as np

#pythran export limit (complex128[])
def limit (x, epsilon=1e-6):
    out = np.empty (shape=x.shape, dtype=x.dtype)
    #out = np.empty_like (x, dtype=x.dtype)
    mask1 = np.abs(x) < epsilon
    out[mask1] = complex(0)
    mask2 = np.logical_not(mask1)
    out[mask2] = x[mask2] / np.abs(x[mask2])
    return out

#pythran export clip(complex128[], float64)
def clip (z, _max):
    mask = np.abs(z) > _max
## print ('#clipped:', np.sum(mask))
    z[mask] = (limit (z[mask]) * _max)
    return z
```

issue_title: Pause in _retry_read_url should only happens from the second time
issue_body:
Hi,


I noticed in `utils.py` in `_retry_read_url` this

    def _retry_read_url(url, retry_count, pause, name):
        """
        Open url (and retry)
        """
        for _ in range(retry_count):
            time.sleep(pause)
            ...

So an unnecessary pause occured from begining (first request)

we might have something like:


    def _retry_read_url(url, retry_count, pause, name):
        """
        Open url (and retry)
        """
        for i in range(retry_count):
            if i != 0:
                time.sleep(pause)
            ...

Misleading "Daily Trading Volume" Plot
issue_body:
Right now the DTV plot created by `plot_daily_volume` only plots days where there was indeed trading volume, and connects those plotted days with line making it seem like trading volume linearly changed between days. Leads to a non-sense tear like this:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1314176/35826565-1a80608e-0a87-11e8-9789-9c2757900b77.png)

As a fix, I added the below code here: 

```
txn_day_amounts = amounts.groupby(amounts.index).sum()
start = txn_day_amounts.index[0]
end = txn_day_amounts.index[-1]
filled_index = pd.date_range(start, end)
daily_amounts = txn_day_amounts.reindex(filled_index).fillna(0) 
```

here in the file
https://github.com/quantopian/pyfolio/blob/0332bb1600c955a09a581c845fed1a8483b30dc1/pyfolio/txn.py#L105

Could make a PR if that helps.

Should be: mean(daily returns - risk_free) / std(daily returns - risk_free) * np.sqrt(252)

CC @justinlent

issue_title: empty json body ignored
issue_body:
Setting an empty json object (which is valid json) is not parsed as json by responses:
```
responses.add(
            responses.POST,
            url,
            json={},
            status=201
        )
```
In `responses.RequestsMock.add`:
```
        # if we were passed a `json` argument,
        # override the body and content_type
        if json:
            body = json_module.dumps(json)
            content_type = 'application/json'
```
Should probably be:
```
        # if we were passed a `json` argument,
        # override the body and content_type
        if json is not None:
            body = json_module.dumps(json)
            content_type = 'application/json'
```

issue_title: dict.pop() leads to inconsistent variables in stack trace
issue_body: If you do a dict.pop() later in the stack trace and that dict is one of the variables in the function higher up in the stack, that object no longer shows up even though the pop is obviously in the lower level.

* fa-keyboard-return
* fa-keyboard-shift
* fa-keyboard-capslock
* fa-keyboard-alt
* fa-keyboard-ctrl
* fa-keyboard-windows
* fa-keyboard-command
* fa-keyboard-fn
* fa-keyboard-tab

Perhaps there could even be two versions of each; one with just the symbol and one with the symbol inside a coloured box to represent the physical key.

Hi, What I’m going for is a series of icons that best represent the 3 ‘main’ Statuses within a Sprint (Agile) board world. 
1.	“Pending” Waiting to start (Sprint Backlog)
2.	“WIP” Work underway, doing
3.	“Done” Work complete
So I think a stop-watch (which you do not have) best represents the Time element for all 3 statuses with the central dial representing states of completion. Additionally, adding colour to each icon can further affirm the meaning of the icon.

I did consider using the “Hourglass” series of icons but didn’t feel they were clear enough. I also considered the Pause/Stop/Run (Video Player icons), but felt Pause didn’t represent “Pending” status clearly enough and Stop doesn’t mean “Finished”

I’m sure the designers can make the icon look more like a stop-watch than a diamond ring, which is what my wife mistook it for LOL.

You guys are awesome love your work!

issue_title: Changing icons at page load (DOMContentLoaded is still too soon)
issue_body: I have an app that can display one of several different icons when the page is refreshed, depending on what was shown just before the user refreshed. The default html contains a generic icon, and the proper icon name to replace it is kept in sessionStorage, which is examined on document.ready. The problem is that the method recommended in the font awesome documentation (searching for "[data-fa-i2svg]") doesn't work during document.ready, since the svg has not yet been created. But I also could not get it to work during the DOMContentLoaded event (as said documentation demonstrates), because the svg still has not been created at that point. (Note that the font awesome documentation example attaches a handler to a button during DOMContentLoaded, but I want to do the icon replacement automatically, without waiting for user to click a button.) I guess what I need is some way to tell when the svg has been created, and react to that.
