issue_title: Can't load system if two files have the same name
issue_body:
Hi there!

I really enjoy OpenFisca, but I recently encountered an issue.

### Here is what I did:
Last week, I was in France countryside and I pair-programmed to code a local benefit.

I did not manage to show our product with all the extensions activated because I had an exception when loading the French tax benefit system.

To demo the issue, I created [guillett/test_openfisca_extensions](https://github.com/guillett/test_openfisca_extensions).

I managed to investigate the issue today and I think I found the culprit line.
#### [openfisca_core/taxbenefitsystems.py:152](https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-core/blob/master/openfisca_core/taxbenefitsystems.py#L152)
```python
    def add_variables_from_file(self, file_path):
        """
        Adds all OpenFisca variables contained in a given file to the tax and benefit system.
        """
        try:
            file_name = path.splitext(path.basename(file_path))[0]
            module_name = '{}_{}'.format(id(self), file_name)  # If two tax and benefit systems load the same module, the second one should not replace the first one. Hence this unique module name.
            module_directory = path.dirname(file_path)
            try:
                module = load_module(module_name, *find_module(file_name, [module_directory]))
            except NameError as e:
                logging.error(str(e) + ": if this code used to work, this error might be due to a major change in OpenFisca-Core. Checkout the changelog to learn more: <https://github.com/openfisca/openfisca-core/blob/master/CHANGELOG.md>")
                raise
            potential_variables = [getattr(module, item) for item in dir(module) if not item.startswith('__')]
            for pot_variable in potential_variables:
                # We only want to get the module classes defined in this module (not imported)
                if isclass(pot_variable) and \
                        issubclass(pot_variable, Variable) and \
                        pot_variable.__module__.endswith(module_name): #   <----------- THIS LINE HERE
                    self.add_variable(pot_variable)
        except:
            log.error(u'Unable to load OpenFisca variables from file "{}"'.format(file_path))
raise
```

## `pot_variable.__module__.endswith(module_name)`

If two modules have the same names (ie. two files) that will fails.

I managed to [replicate this issue](https://circleci.com/gh/openfisca/country-template/239) with less complexity (ie. only with the country-template package).


## Context

I identify more as a:

- Developer _(I create tools that use the existing OpenFisca code)_.

issue_title: issue.update doesn't work without hackery
issue_body:
```
i.update(assignee={'name':'foo'})
...
JIRAError: HTTP 400: "one of 'fields' or 'update' required"
http://jiraserver/jira/rest/api/2/issue/497258
```

```
i.update(fields={'assignee':{'name':'foo'}})
...
JIRAError: HTTP 400: "one of 'fields' or 'update' required"
http://jiraserver/jira/rest/api/2/issue/497258
```

```
i.update(fields={'fields':{'assignee':{'name':'foo'}}})
...
# works
```

JIRA server in this case is running 6.3.x.  As far as I can tell, this syntax structure has been required ever since the issue PUT REST API was added.

issue_title: RuntimeConfig: Unable to retrieve "text" value of the Variable
issue_body:
### Description
The runtimeconfig.v1beta1.variable needs to be updated with "text" property so users will be able to access the text value.

### Steps to reproduce with code samples
For creating the config with the name of my-config
`gcloud beta runtime-config configs create my-config`

For creating the key value pair, key is "some-gibberish" and value is "hahahablblblbl"
`gcloud beta runtime-config configs variables set some-gibberish \`
    `'hahahablblblbl' --config-name my-config`

Now enter the Python command line by typing 'python' in the terminal and then hit enter.
Run following simple Python snippet

`from google.cloud import runtimeconfig`
`client = runtimeconfig.Client()`
`config = client.config('my-config')`
`var = config.get_variable('some-gibberish')`

Expected output:
`>>> var.value`

But what if we don't need the base64 encoding option given in the [documentation](https://cloud.google.com/deployment-manager/runtime-configurator/set-and-get-variables#creatingvariable)?

So we're going to change this gcloud command to store the value of "some-gibberish" as text
`gcloud beta runtime-config configs variables set some-gibberish \`
     `'hahahablblblbl' --config-name my-config --is-text`

Exit the python command line

Run the gcloud command with `--is-text` flag

Go to the python command line again, execute the simple Python snippet from earlier.

Output for var.value
`>>> var.value`
`>>>`

Output for var.text
`>>> var.text`
`Traceback (most recent call last):`
 ` File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>`
`AttributeError: 'Variable' object has no attribute 'text'`
`>>>`

### Effect
For now this would mean that there's no way to retrieve the text values in Python which would force users to store everything as base64 encoded values.

issue_title: Django freezes when using Stackdriver logging
issue_labels: [{'id': 311535631, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/api:%20logging', 'name': 'api: logging', 'color': 'c0c0c0', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604244, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/priority:%20p1', 'name': 'priority: p1', 'color': 'ffa03e', 'default': False}, {'id': 544604235, 'url': 'https://api.github.com/repos/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python/labels/status:%20acknowledged', 'name': 'status: acknowledged', 'color': 'fef2c0', 'default': False}]
issue_body:
OS: `MacOS 10.11.6`
Python: `Python 2.7.10`
Package: 
```
Name: google-cloud-logging
Version: 0.23.1
Summary: Python Client for Stackdriver Logging
Home-page: https://github.com/GoogleCloudPlatform/google-cloud-python
Author: Google Cloud Platform
Author-email: jjg+google-cloud-python@google.com
License: Apache 2.0
Location: /Users/briandeheus/.virtualenvs/proj/lib/python2.7/site-packages
Requires: gapic-google-cloud-logging-v2, google-cloud-core, grpcio
```

## Steps to reproduce

1. start a new Django project
2. set up your logging as follows:
```
LOGGING = {
    'version': 1,
    'handlers': {
        'stackdriver': {
            'class': 'google.cloud.logging.handlers.CloudLoggingHandler',
            'client': logging.Client()
        }
    },
    'loggers': {
        '': {
            'handlers': ['stackdriver'],
            'level': 'INFO',
            'propagate': True,
        }
}
```

Your app will freeze after a few seconds. I've narrowed it down by adding some print statements to the stackdriver module:

```
_start: Starting
_start: Started
_Run: RUN WORKER
_Run: NOT STOPPING
_Run: WAIT
[2017-03-02 08:18:44,595] DEBUG Skipping environment variable credential check because profile name was explicitly set.
Enqueue: Acquiring
Enqueue: Acquired
Enqueue: Notifying
Enqueue: Notified
Enqueue: Releasing
Enqueue: Released
[2017-03-02 08:18:44,595] DEBUG Looking for credentials via: env
_Run: COMMITING
Commit: BATCH COMMIT
 Enqueue: Acquiring
Commit: WRITE ENTRIES
```

The Worker class has some modified methods to achieve this:

```python
    def _run(self):
        """The entry point for the worker thread.

        Loops until ``stopping`` is set to :data:`True`, and commits batch
        entries written during :meth:`enqueue`.
        """
        try:
            self._entries_condition.acquire()
            self.started = True
            print "_Run: RUN WORKER"
            while not self.stopping:
                print "_Run: NOT STOPPING"
                if len(self.batch.entries) == 0:
                    print "_Run: WAIT"
                    # branch coverage of this code extremely flaky
                    self._entries_condition.wait()  # pragma: NO COVER

                if len(self.batch.entries) > 0:
                    print "_Run: COMMITING"
                    self.batch.commit()

            print "_Run: STOPPING"
        finally:
            print "_Run: RELEASING"
            self._entries_condition.release()

        # main thread may be waiting for worker thread to finish writing its
        # final entries. here we signal that it's done.
        self._stop_condition.acquire()
        self._stop_condition.notify()
        self._stop_condition.release()

    def _start(self):
        """Called by this class's constructor

        This method is responsible for starting the thread and registering
        the exit handlers.
        """
        try:
            print "_start: Starting"
            self._entries_condition.acquire()
            self._thread = threading.Thread(
                target=self._run, name=self.name)
            self._thread.setDaemon(False)
            self._thread.start()
            print "_start: Started"
        finally:
            self._entries_condition.release()
            atexit.register(self._stop)

    def enqueue(self, record, message):
        """Queues up a log entry to be written by the background thread."""
        try:
            print "Enqueue: Acquiring"
            self._entries_condition.acquire()
            if self.stopping:
                print "Enqueue: Stopping"
                return
            print "Enqueue: Acquired"
            info = {'message': message, 'python_logger': record.name}
            self.batch.log_struct(info, severity=record.levelname)
            print "Enqueue: Notifying"
            self._entries_condition.notify()
            print "Enqueue: Notified"
        finally:
            print "Enqueue: Releasing"
            self._entries_condition.release()
            print "Enqueue: Released"
```

I've tried both the `SyncTransport` and the `BackgroundThreadTransport` but both lock up. Whether running your project in the Django development mode or using uWSGI, the end result is always the same.

issue_title: Documentation is misleading
issue_body:
The documentation is a bit misleading, particularly for beginners.  Instructing people to run this command if the file exists:

```
$ rm app/assets/stylesheets/application.css
```

Will delete the existence of that file, along with what it is doing:

```
/*
 * This is a manifest file that'll be compiled into application.css, which will include all the files
 * listed below.
 *
 * Any CSS and SCSS file within this directory, lib/assets/stylesheets, vendor/assets/stylesheets,
 * or vendor/assets/stylesheets of plugins, if any, can be referenced here using a relative path.
 *
 * You're free to add application-wide styles to this file and they'll appear at the top of the
 * compiled file, but it's generally better to create a new file per style scope.
 *
 *= require_self
 *= require_tree .
 */
```

Really what you want to do is rename the file `app/assets/stylesheets/application.css` to `app/assets/stylesheets/application.css.scss` in order to access the full power of SASS, while maintaining the way the asset pipeline compiles assets, rather than eliminating everything entirely.

issue_title: Control which internal targets are included as source vs binary jar within generated idea project
issue_body:
The [pants `idea` goal](http://pantsbuild.github.io/goals_reference.html?highlight=idea#gref-phase-idea) is currently used to construct an intellij idea project for one or more targets. The source of target dependencies (both direct and transitive) which are "internal" to the source tree are pulled into the generated project. In a monorepo, this might result in a project with a large number of sources, which can slow down indexing, compilation, etc.

Proposal: Add a mechanism to control which direct internal dependencies (and their transitive dependencies) are included by default as source within the generated project, versus pre-compiled jars. This might look similar to the [`with_sources` flag](http://pantsbuild.github.io/build_dictionary.html#with_sources), but could be applied to pants deps to mark them as "jar only" for purposes of ide project generation. The syntax might look like the following:

In `src/scala/com/example/C/BUILD`:

```
scala_library(name='C',
  deps=[
    pants('src/scala/com/example/A'), # sources included in project
    pants('src/scala/com/example/B').jar_only(), # binary jar built and referenced by project
  ],
)
```

When the `idea` goal is executed for target `src/scala/com/example/C`, a binary jar would be created for target `src/scala/com/example/B` and the idea project for `C` would reference the jar instead of the sources for `B`.

The `jar_only` flag would only be honored for direct dependencies of the specific targets passed to the `idea` goal, to avoid having the flags for one target conflict with those of some other target.

issue_title: Document how task docstrings are expected to be formatted in the development docs
issue_body:
Pants' convention is to have a short summary line, followed optionally by a blank line and a larger description. But, I couldn't find that documented on the pants website quickly.

good examples
```
class T(Task):
  """A task that does something"""

class T(Task):
  """A task that does something

     Further explanation of what the task does.
     Possibly on multiple lines.
  """
```

Bad
```
class T(Task):
  """A task that does something. Or another thing, and with a really long first line that gets into too many details and will totally wrap on the terminal making it difficult to read"""


class T(Task):
  """A task that does something. 
     Or another thing, and doesn't have a blank line in the expected place."""


class T(Task):
  """
     A task that does something, but has an empty first line."""
```

issue_title: The generated coveragerc seems wrong
issue_body:
I've encountered the case where the generated coveragerc looks like this:

```
[run]
branch = True
timid = True

[report]
exclude_lines =
        def __repr__
        raise NotImplementedError

[paths]
shared =
                shared
                .pants.d/pyprep/sources/7612f833acaeb440ee7d4249c5c2c340885c1ec7
                /usr/local/google/home/tanin/projects/clusterfuzz-tools/shared
                /usr/local/google/home/tanin/projects/clusterfuzz-tools/.pants.d/pyprep/sources/7612f833acaeb440ee7d4249c5c2c340885c1ec7
error =
                error
                .pants.d/pyprep/sources/7612f833acaeb440ee7d4249c5c2c340885c1ec7
                /usr/local/google/home/tanin/projects/clusterfuzz-tools/error
                /usr/local/google/home/tanin/projects/clusterfuzz-tools/.pants.d/pyprep/sources/7612f833acaeb440ee7d4249c5c2c340885c1ec7
```

(The content is generated from this line: https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/python/tasks2/pytest_run.py#L210)

The run command is: `./pants test.pytest --coverage=auto error:test -- -p no:logging`

And here's the BUILD:

```
python_tests(
    name='test',
    sources=rglobs('tests/*.py'),
    coverage='error',
    compatibility=['>=2.7','<3'],
    dependencies=[
        ':src',
        '//shared:test_libs',
        '//3rdparty/python:pyfakefs',
        '//3rdparty/python:mock'
    ]
)
```

`:src` and `//shared:test_libs` are regular `python_library`s. `:src` has the module `error`.

This leads to the generated report below:

```
18:05:50 00:03       [coverage]
                     Name                           Stmts   Miss Branch BrPart  Cover
                     ----------------------------------------------------------------
                     shared/test_libs/__init__.py       0      0      0      0   100%
                     shared/test_libs/helpers.py       38     38      8      0     0%
                     ----------------------------------------------------------------
                     TOTAL                             38     38      8      0     0%
```

You can see that it doesn't include the module `error`. If I remove `//shared:test_libs` from the BUILD target, it suddenly shows the coverage from the module `error`.

After testing, there's something strange around `[paths]`. I'm not sure.

This is Pants 1.3.0.

issue_title: Filespecs generated for `zglobs` do not match twitter.commons
issue_body:
For `zglobs('**/a.py')`, the filespecs generation code in `wrapped_globs` generates the filespec `**/a.py`, which will not match the file `a.py`... only files with that name in subdirectories. This is similar to the special case in `RGlobs.to_filespecs` (except that that method additionally generates recursive filespecs).

Matching `twitter.commons` isn't going to be a requirement for very long due to removal of that dependency, but fixing this case might reduce surprise.

issue_title: pants export format currently does not support jars resolved with classifiers
.forward "hangs" in a flask app.
issue_body:
Dear all,

I must admit I'm not too sure about how to help dig deeper into the issue I am facing. Here goes a quick description:
- Running python3.5, keras-mxnet 2.2.0, keras 2.2.2, mxnet 1.0.0 (although I have tried also with mxnet 1.2.1)
- I'm building a flask app for serving the model, written in Keras, which includes a convolutional network for classifying text. Inputs are chars/words/BPE tokens, in one-shot representation, output is a dense layer.
- I have a model defined and loaded outside the predict endpoint, so that subsequent calls to the predict endpoint do not require to reload the model each time.
- When querying the predict endpoint, the first time around everything works fine. The second time, it gets stuck in the predict code and never comes back. Delving deep into the code of keras-mxnet, I found out that it gets stuck in the predict function defined in `_make_predict_function`, in line
```                self._module.forward(batch, is_train=False)```
(line 4840 of the current code, so basically, in the `forward` step). 
Context: https://github.com/awslabs/keras-apache-mxnet/blob/332c62f7b521b109431c0ef91317b2ce00e4cfb7/keras/backend/mxnet_backend.py#L4840

Now. The interesting part is that this problem arises when using the latest Flask (1.0.2). However, using Flask 0.12.4 does not lead to this problem, and the predict endpoint can be queried as often as required.

We had a similar problem in the past using Keras with a TensorFlow backend, although that seemed to be due to session problems, and that's why we switched to MXNet. 

I'm not providing a simple code to reproduce the problem because, this being included in a Flask app, I'm not too sure what a "minimal code" would be, aside from the full code base.

Any clues regarding what might be going on?

Thank you very much in advance!

issue_title: Fail to apply different Lookup Transformers to same feature
issue_body:
We want to be able to apply different Lookup transformations to the same categorical feature.
In our case, we want to apply a mapping that uses an average and another that uses the standard deviation and generate two different DerivedFields.

When we do it, the conversion to PMML fails because it tries to create two derived fields with the same name: "lookup(<feature_name>)". The error message is:

```
java.lang.IllegalArgumentException: lookup(<feature_name>)
	at org.jpmml.converter.PMMLEncoder.checkName(PMMLEncoder.java:233)
	at org.jpmml.converter.PMMLEncoder.addDerivedField(PMMLEncoder.java:127)
	at org.jpmml.converter.PMMLEncoder.createDerivedField(PMMLEncoder.java:137)
	at sklearn2pmml.preprocessing.LookupTransformer.encodeFeatures(LookupTransformer.java:134)
	at sklearn_pandas.DataFrameMapper.initializeFeatures(DataFrameMapper.java:75)
	at sklearn.Initializer.encodeFeatures(Initializer.java:53)
```

I would expect `PMMLEncoder.addDerivedField` to generate a new Derived Field Name (e.g. append "_v2") when it finds an existing field with the same name.

Is there any way to change this behaviour?
Or is there a workaround? For instance apply a Dummy Transformer to rename the initial field before applying the second LookupTransformer?

Related to issue: https://github.com/jpmml/sklearn2pmml/issues/70

issue_title: etree serializer breaks if an etree.Entity is present
issue_body:
The etree serializer breaks if an Entity object is present. To reproduce: import html5lib from html5lib.serializer import HTMLSerializer import lxml from lxml import etree def render(element, treeType, implementation=None): walker_cls = html5lib.getTreeWalker(treeType, implementation=implementation) walker = walker_cls(element) serializer = HTMLSerializer() print(serializer.render(walker)) elements = html5lib.parseFragment('
', treebuilder='lxml') element, = elements element.append(etree.Entity('amp')) # Works, outputs: #
&
render(element, 'lxml') # Fails with the exception below. render(element, 'etree', implementation=lxml.etree) Exception: Traceback (most recent call last): File "test-html5lib.py", line 25, in render(element, 'etree', implementation=lxml.etree) File "test-html5lib.py", line 16, in render print(serializer.render(walker)) File "/.../python3.6/site-packages/html5lib/serializer.py", line 323, in render return "".join(list(self.serialize(treewalker))) File "/.../python3.6/site-packages/html5lib/serializer.py", line 209, in serialize for token in treewalker: File "/.../python3.6/site-packages/html5lib/filters/optionaltags.py", line 18, in __iter__ for previous, token, next in self.slider(): File "/.../python3.6/site-packages/html5lib/filters/optionaltags.py", line 9, in slider for token in self.source: File "/.../python3.6/site-packages/html5lib/treewalkers/base.py", line 94, in __iter__ details = self.getNodeDetails(currentNode) File "/.../python3.6/site-packages/html5lib/treewalkers/etree.py", line 61, in getNodeDetails assert isinstance(node.tag, string_types), type(node.tag) AssertionError: I believe this is because etree.py's `TreeWalker` class's [`getNodeDetails()` method](https://github.com/html5lib/html5lib-python/blob/984f934d30ba2fde0e9ce5d4581c73fb81654474/html5lib/treewalkers/etree.py#L39) does not have logic like the [following in `etree_lxml.py`](https://github.com/html5lib/html5lib-python/blob/984f934d30ba2fde0e9ce5d4581c73fb81654474/html5lib/treewalkers/etree_lxml.py#L155): elif node.tag == etree.Entity: return base.ENTITY, ensure_str(node.text)[1:-1] # strip &;

issue_title: Fatal errors with current version (NameError: global name 'expected_state' is not defined)
issue_body:
Thanks for a handy script -- we've been using it and find it useful.

Alas, the latest version that gets installed via pip is fatally broken -- this was working a few days ago, so I dug in to see what I could find.

Starting at https://github.com/skymill/aws-ec2-assign-elastic-ip/blob/fd2208b7dcfc3f7dc1b7563c1c4f7219bf553ae1/aws_ec2_assign_elastic_ip/__init__.py#L171

This line returns an undefined variable, causing the script to fail. expected_state isn't defined anywhere other than line 171 and 181; in the older versions, line 171 would have returned True and line 181 would have returned False.

Also, once this is fixed, there is another bug: args.invalid_ips will be Null unless the script is called with a value passed in via --invalid-ips. This causes args.invalid_ips to be Null, which causes _is_ip_in_range to return False, which causes _is_valid to return False, which means the entire script fails to assign an elastic IP unless --invalid-ips is specified on the command line.

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/bin/aws-ec2-assign-elastic-ip", line 31, in <module>
    aws_ec2_assign_elastic_ip.main()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/aws_ec2_assign_elastic_ip/__init__.py", line 60, in main
    address = _get_unassociated_address()
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/aws_ec2_assign_elastic_ip/__init__.py", line 127, in _get_unassociated_address
    if _is_valid(address.public_ip):
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/aws_ec2_assign_elastic_ip/__init__.py", line 191, in _is_valid
    if _is_ip_in_range(address, args.valid_ips):
  File "/usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages/aws_ec2_assign_elastic_ip/__init__.py", line 171, in _is_ip_in_range
    return expected_state
NameError: global name 'expected_state' is not defined

issue_title: resolution needed: handling of *.cmap.txt files (and the information they contain)
issue_body:
marekjez86 wrote to @JelleBosmaMT: This delivery also had src/NotoSansDevanagari/NotoSansDevanagariUI.cmap.txt . I have never seen it in the delivery. It is not referenced explicitly. Is this intentional?

The text below was copied from @JelleBosmaMT 's response e-mail:


[...]
The cmap file has been part of all previous Devanagari deliveries:
starting with the trial data with 2 and 4 masters.

I noted that it was missing from the GitHub repository when I was pointed
towards it in order to get the .plist package list, which I was expected
to update for the new file names. On the 2nd of June I posted the
following message about the format of the package list. I did not see a
response, which also mentions the missing cmap file:


On 02-jun.-16 15:10, @JelleBosmaMT wrote:

>Hi,
>
>
>I prepared a set deliverables of Noto Sans Devanagari and Sinhala. As a
>test our QE team is expected to generate fonts from the source files with
>the pipeline or fontmake as it seems to be called. Therefore I was asked
>to provide a new .plist file based
>on one that can be found on GitHub
>(https://github.com/googlei18n/fontmake/tree/master/test/NotoSansDevanagar
>i).
>
>
>In the package list the OTL source files are assigned to the masters in
>the .glyphs file. I did not think that would work, and it turns out it
>doesn’t. One cannot assign the OTL source files of the UI font to the UI
>masters as the sample file does, because
>there are no UI masters. And although makefont generates fonts with the
>OTL sources assigned to the masters that do exist, this produces  fonts
>that are simply 8 fonts with the outlines from the 8 masters with OTL
>tables of the document fonts. That is of course
>very different from what is intended.
>
>
>The OTL sources have only an indirect relation ship with the masters. The
>.glyphs files define one or more font families with a range of widths and
>weights: the instances. The masters and OTL source files are ingredients
>that go into these font families.
>The link between master, OTL source and these families with instances are
>the interpolation values.
>
>
>So I propose to modify the .plist to assign the OTL source files to
>Family name + interpolation values:
>
>
><key>NotoSansDevanagari-SemiBold</key>
>Should refer to the font family Noto Sans Devanagari with interpolation
>values weight = 151 and width = 100
>
>
>The <key>NotoSansDevanagariUI-SemiBold</key> which currently points to
>nothing, would have to point to the font family Noto Sans Devanagari UI.
>
>
>I assume XML experts have more advanced ways of expressing this, I am
>just trying something to make my point, that OTL sources are assigned to
>interpolation values of font families:
>
>
>
><key>Noto Sans Devanagari</key>
><dict>
>...
><key>151 100</key>
><dict>
><key>GDEF</key>
><string>Noto Sans Devanagari GDEF.txt</string>
><key>GPOS</key>
><string>Noto Sans Devanagari 151-100 GPOS.txt</string>
><key>GSUB</key>
><string>Noto Sans Devanagari 151-100 GSUB.txt</string>
></dict>
>
>…
></dict>
>
><key>Noto Sans Devanagari UI</key>
><dict>
>...
><key>151 100</key>
><dict>
><key>GDEF</key>
><string>Noto Sans Devanagari UI GDEF.txt</string>
><key>GPOS</key>
><string>Noto Sans Devanagari UI 151-100 GPOS.txt</string>
><key>GSUB</key>
><string>Noto Sans Devanagari UI 151-100 GSUB.txt</string>
><key>cmap</key>
><string>NotoSansDevanagariUI.cmap</string>
>
></dict>
>
>…
></dict>
>

>
>I could not help noticing that in the GitHub page with test data for Noto
>Sans Devanagari the provided source file for the cmap of the UI fonts is
>missing. Of course the cmap files would have to be added to the updated
>format of the package list.
>

issue_title: Deis Update Client
issue_body:
The Deis Update engine should be a small, simple client that can be enabled on a Deis Cluster to provide automatic update functionality on a cluster level.

This should use the upgrade-prep and upgrade-takeover functionality in deisctl, and the CoreOS omaha server on the other end. It should ship as a fleet unit, and be probably be initially disabled by default until it is mature.

Related:
https://github.com/deis/deis/issues/2106
https://coreos.com/docs/coreupdate/custom-apps/coreupdate-protocol/

issue_title: [incr tsdb()] profile reading/writing depends on locale encoding
issue_body: Both ItsdbProfile and TestSuite rely on the locale (e.g., terminal) encoding for reading/writing profiles, and this might not be the same as the files. Add an explicit `encoding` parameter with a default of `utf-8` to the relevant functions/methods. This should make things better without changing the current behavior (for whom it currently works, unless they are reading/writing non-ascii non-utf-8 profiles).

issue_title: Uploading environment to Anaconda cloud, fails with 400
issue_body:
Hi,

I'm following the instructions here[1].  These are the two commands that docs say can be used to upload the environment.

>conda env export -n my-environment
>conda env upload my-environment

The second command fails with:
>CondaEnvRuntimeError: Runtime error: Unable to locate environment file: environment.yml

It seems that it's expecting the first command to write the output to environment.yml, however it just writes it to stdout. This is easily fixed by redirecting stdout to environment.yml.

Once that is done, and the upload command is run again. I get a 400 response. Investigating further, the specific error is is this:
>requirements, announce and description are required

The root of the problem seems to be the following. `conda_env.utils.Uploader.upload()` first checks that the packagename already exists (via `is_ready()`). If it doesn't exist, it calls `self.binstar.add_release(self.username, self.packagename, self.version, {}, '', '')`

Those last three arguments of that method are named `requirements`, `description`, and `release_attrs`. I replaced that call with the following:

`self.binstar.add_release(self.username, self.packagename, self.version, {'python': '2.7'}, 'blah', {'description': 'foobar'})`

I just guessed what actual params to use here. Anyway, that failed with a 404 (I didn't step through and investigate), but a subsequent call did work, and my environment was uploaded successfully.

I'm not too aufait with Anaconda Cloud, but it seems like everything is a 'package' (even environments), and the server needs those fields to be populated.

These are the versions of anaconda-client and conda:  
>anaconda-client           1.6.3
>conda                     4.3.16

I suspect it's either PEBKAC or I'm running old versions of anaconda-client (even though conda tells me it is up to date), otherwise more people would be having this problem I suspect.

Cheers

1. https://docs.continuum.io/anaconda-cloud/using#environments

issue_title: updating/installing nb_conda, nb_conda_kernels, nb_anacondacloud does not always make python3 kernels available in python2 envs
issue_body:
I was trying to get a python3 kernel working inside a python2 environment (testing the compatibility of building the jupyter notebook documentation which includes notebooks that use python3 kernels). 

Following http://ipython.readthedocs.io/en/stable/install/kernel_install.html#kernels-for-python-2-and-3 did not solve the problem & though it apparently installed a python3 kernel according to the kernelspec list. 

I arrived at http://stackoverflow.com/questions/38257138/jupyter-no-such-kernel-named-python3 (specifically http://stackoverflow.com/a/38705741/1816995) but this also did not work. 

Additionally, I have a (pip based) editable install of the notebook, and when installing/updating `nb_conda`, `nb_conda_kernels`, and `nb_anacondacloud` it did not recognise this, but instead created a hard dependency on notebook 4.2.3. This doesn't seem to shadow my editable install when I open a python or IPython interpreter, but I could see that that may be part of what is causing the problem.

If it *had* shadowed my install, attempting to `conda uninstall notebook` also uninstalls `nb_conda`, `nb_conda_kernels`, and `nb_anacondacloud` meaning that the SO solution wouldn't have worked.

NB: Eventually I was able to get this to work after manually deleting & recreating the kernelspec file, but I figured that this was enough of a pain that you should be made aware (especially if it was at all related to the shadowing).

issue_title: Due to glibc 2.12 limitation, static executables that use time(), cpuinfo() and maybe a few others cannot be run on systems that do not support or use `vsyscall=emulate`
issue_body:
The conda gcc package (and probably multiple other packages) segfault when used on newer Debian and when running under Linux on Windows emulation.

This is because the conda gcc package is linked against a very old version of glibc (older than 2.14 which was released in 2011!). This old version of glibc uses the ["vsyscall" functionality](https://lwn.net/Articles/446528/) which is not available on new Linux distributions and on Linux on Windows. 

vsyscall was deprecated in Linux in 2011 (!!). Debian has now disable emulation in the kernel and this causes all apps to crash when started. It is also considered insecure to have it enabled.

Newer glibc versions (2.14) have removed the usage of this functionality.

issue_title: Excessive logging from urllib3.util.retry
issue_body:
Hello!

I'm using Requests in my project, and have set up retries using urllib3's retry util. To monitor the retries, I've configured that modules logger as it logs useful information.

Unfortunately, this also generates a lot of less useful logs, which are triggered by elasticsearch-py.
`elasticsearch.connection.http_urllib3` [always sets](https://github.com/elastic/elasticsearch-py/blob/master/elasticsearch/connection/http_urllib3.py#L171) `retries=False` when calling `urllib3.connectionpool.urlopen`. This causes urllib3 to call the `from_int` class method of the `Retry` class, which ultimately causes the following to be logged: `Converted retries value: False -> Retry(total=False, connect=None, read=None, redirect=0, status=None)`

It would be better to instead to call `urlopen` with a `Retry` object with `total=0`, which will be functionally equivalent but suppress the noisy log message.

I'm going to raise a PR for the update for your consideration.

Thanks!

issue_body:
### Description
Commands such as az iot device create --hub-name milopeiot -d testDevice fail when using the AzureUSGovernment environment. issue_title: [Network] How to attach a NIC to a load balancer's inbound NAT pool?
issue_body:
Is there a sample somewhere of using the inbound NAT pool for a load balancer instance? I looked [here](https://github.com/Azure/azure-cli-samples/blob/master/network/beta-load-balancer.md) and didn't see it (only NAT rules are illustrated). I'm probably missing something, but I haven't been able to figure out how to attach a NIC to a load balancer's inbound NAT pool, or even confirm if I'm thinking about how to use them correctly :)

For example, if I create the following NAT pool, which I hoped to use in order to expose a series of sequential frontend ports that are NAT'd to a single port in a backend address pool:

```shell
az network lb inbound-nat-pool create -g <group> \
  -n <name> \
  --lb-name <lbName> \
  --protocol tcp \
  --frontend-port-range-start 2200 \
  --frontend-port-range-end 2205 \
  --backend-port 22
```

If I try to reference this pool by name, using the either the `--lb-address-pools` or `--lb-inbound-nat-rule` parameter of the `az network nic create` command, it fails to find it. That makes sense I guess, since the former is looking for an address pool, and the later is looking for a NAT *rule*, not a NAT *pool*. However, the `az network nic create` command doesn't have a `--lb-nat-pool(s)` parameter, so I'm not sure how to actually configure the NAT pool.

Additionally, the `az network nic ip-config` command group includes commands for adding/removing a NIC to a address pool or NAT rule, but once again, none of these seem to support a NAT *pool*.

Finally, if I look in the Azure portal underneath the LB instance, I don't actually see any mention of NAT pools anywhere, and the NAT pool I created via the CLI doesn't show up under the `Inbound NAT Rules` blade, so I'm somewhat confused.

I would love any clarification on this. I could achieve what I'm doing above by creating six separate NAT rules, and referencing them via the `--lb-inbound-nat-rule` parameter of `az network nic create`, but I really liked the idea of creating a NAT pool and attaching NICs to it in order to reserve a front-end port. Then again, maybe I'm completely misinterpreting the use of a NAT pool. Thanks!

issue_title: Django rest framework doesn't use has_object_create_permission

I saw in the tests `has_object_create_permission`. I tried this method in my API and found it isn't called, because there is no object when creating an object. Ref. http://www.django-rest-framework.org/api-guide/permissions/

Two things: clarify the test and update the docs?

issue_title: ClientSession.__aexit__ synchronous session close
issue_body:
## Long story short

When using `ClientSession` as an asynchronous context manager, `self.close` is not awaited and it throws `DeprecationWarning`.

## Expected behaviour

Returns `self.close()` in `ClientSession.__aexit__`

## Actual behaviour

Calls `self.close()` in `ClientSession.__aexit__`

## Steps to reproduce

```
import asyncio
from aiohttp import ClientSession

loop = asyncio.get_event_loop()


async def coro():
    async with ClientSession(loop=loop):
        pass


loop.run_until_complete(coro())
```

## Your environment
```
OS X 10.12.3
python 3.6.0
aiohttp==2.2.3
```

issue_title: Questions on documentation/suggestions for improvements
issue_body:
More specific questions from documentation I've looked over:

- http://nbgrader.readthedocs.org/en/stable/user_guide/01_philosophy.html

    - concept of nbgrader file system structure and hierarchy, but no indication I see of where that needs to live and how you reference it across multiple students or multiple instructors.

- http://nbgrader.readthedocs.org/en/stable/user_guide/03_generating_assignments.html

    - mention of needing a "gradebook.db" database, but not specific about where one would put it (implies it is in course folder at same level as "source" folder, I think).
    - code to add assignments to database, and how to then release an assignment, and implication, I believe, that an instructor should run this code as well as the "nbgrader" command in the directory where the "source" directory lives (and it looks like that is the expectation throughout, but I don't think I've seen that explicitly stated).

- http://nbgrader.readthedocs.org/en/stable/user_guide/11_jupyterhub_config.html

    - here, it tells you how to configure an nbgrader_config.py file for formgrade app, but I don't see where one is supposed to put it or how one tells nbgrader where it is.  Makes more sense if each user gets one, or at least you need one in the same folder where you run nbgrader, but doesn't say that.
    - And, if multiple graders, can multiple users log in to an instance of formgrade?  It takes up a port, so if not, per user, would you need separate ports for different instructors, or to coordinate so only one person using at a time?
    - c.HubAuth.notebook_url_prefix - the notes on this imply that it will be relative to the current user's home directory - is this to guide the formgrade app to the course folder?

        - So does this imply that each grader who might use this app will need at least a symbolic link to the course folder in their home folder?
        - Could I make a standard nbgrader_config.py that I put in the course folder that anyone with access could use to launch the formgrader?

- http://nbgrader.readthedocs.org/en/stable/user_guide/07_releasing_assignments.html

    - here it talks about more nbgrader_config.py that will get automatically loaded when we run nbgrader, but it contains different information than is in the nbgrader_config.py in 11_jupyterhub_config.
    - it also doesn't say explicitly where that file needs to live, or where you are running the nbgrader command from (from the directory that holds the nbgrader file system hierarchy?).
    - Sounds like it assumes a single instructor, also?

- http://nbgrader.readthedocs.org/en/stable/user_guide/08_fetching_assignments.html

    - what is the default exchange directory?
    - looks like each student will need an nbgrader_config.py file - will it just live in their home directory?  Can you change it so that they at least are in a child directory, so they don't clutter the home directory?
    how do the commands a student runs know where the database is?

- http://nbgrader.readthedocs.org/en/stable/user_guide/10_collecting_assignments.html

    - how would an instructor/grader's nbgrader_config.py file need to be configured for this to work?  Would they need the same explicit definition of the exchange directory, and then need to run the nbgrader command in the course directory?

issue_title: clear_output message should support clearing last output_area
issue_body:
Currently, `clear_output` message will ultimately clear all the `output_area` under a cell. It would be beneficial to have an option on the message so that it clear the last `output_area` div generated under the cell. For example: If we have the following structure under a cell due to a series of `display_data` messages: ```
First
Second
Third
Fourth
``` Sending a `clear_output` with a special flag (lets call it `incremental`) will yield: ```
First
Second
Third
``` The limitation of `clear_output` is better appreciated when using jupyter-incubator/declarativewidgets. Since declarativewidgets are renderd in the `output_area`, taking an action that calls a function that has `display.clear_output()` will end up erasing the widgets as well. I would think that once `ipywidgets` move their content into `output-area`, they will encounter scenarios where they are affected by this. ![clearoutput](https://cloud.githubusercontent.com/assets/2474841/13256905/2b978e6c-da12-11e5-8b2c-540903bd103e.gif) /cc @jdfreder @SylvainCorlay

issue_title: New demo app for getting started guide
issue_body:
Vi trenger en enkel demo app som brukes gjennom getting started guiden som eksempel. Altså en app som begynner enkelt, men har rom for funksjonalitet der vi får vist frem mer kompliserte ting lenger ut i getting started guiden.

Jeg tenker en enkel twitter klon der vi har en “Account” modell med en eller flere brukere. Account modellen blir da det vi bruker som rolle i cradmin. Så har vi et admin UI der man kan legge til og administrere “tweets” og et publicui der man kan bla og søke i tweets.

Altså kan jo du begynne med å legge inn en ny django app (som heter cradmin_gettingstarted) i demo/ og lage modellene:

- Account
- AccountAdministrator (som har foreignkey til settings.AUTH_USER_MODEL og til Account modellen)

Deretter kan du sette opp en cradmin instance for account admins i den django appen. Når vi har en minimal versjon på plass kan vi begynne å utvide demoappen og guiden hånd i hånd med ulike temaer (listviews, forms, editviews, createviews, deleteviews, …)

issue_title: support for np.<ufunc>(..., dtype=...)
issue_body:
Ufuncs support passing a dtype kwarg to pick the output value.  This is particularly useful for casting the output to complex.

For example, the function `lambda x: np.real_if_close(np.cos(np.arccos(x, dtype=complex) / 3))` is real valued for `x>=-1`, despite the complex intermediate value.  Currently, autograd errors on trying to differentiate such a function.  It is possibly to workaround this by removing the `dtype` kwarg and explicitly passing in a complex argument to `grad(func)`, but more direct support would be appreciated.

Alternatively, support for terms of the form `x = x.astype(complex)` would already help (one would just have to call that before calling the ufunc).

issue_title: Reading unexpected attribute type crashes
issue_body:
I encountered this when I tried to read the "X.509 Certificate Issuer" attribute with PyKMIP, which seems (as best I can tell) to not be supported, so when PyKMIP goes to parse the response it errors out in an unfortunate way. This manifests in the following backtrace:

```
  File "pykmip/pykmip_tests.py", line 259, in cert_test
    a = client.get_attributes(id, [attr])
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/PyKMIP-0.8.dev0-py3.6.egg/kmip/pie/client.py", line 39, in wrapper
    return function(self, *args, **kwargs)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/PyKMIP-0.8.dev0-py3.6.egg/kmip/pie/client.py", line 841, in get_attributes
    result = self.proxy.get_attributes(uid, attribute_names)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/PyKMIP-0.8.dev0-py3.6.egg/kmip/services/kmip_client.py", line 590, in get_attributes
    response = self._send_and_receive_message(request)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/PyKMIP-0.8.dev0-py3.6.egg/kmip/services/kmip_client.py", line 1563, in _send_and_receive_message
    response.read(data)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/PyKMIP-0.8.dev0-py3.6.egg/kmip/core/messages/messages.py", line 385, in read
    batch_item.read(istream)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/PyKMIP-0.8.dev0-py3.6.egg/kmip/core/messages/messages.py", line 294, in read
    self.response_payload.read(tstream)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/PyKMIP-0.8.dev0-py3.6.egg/kmip/core/messages/payloads/get_attributes.py", line 299, in read
    attribute.read(tstream)
  File "/usr/lib/python3.6/site-packages/PyKMIP-0.8.dev0-py3.6.egg/kmip/core/objects.py", line 118, in read
    self.attribute_value.tag = Tags.ATTRIBUTE_VALUE
AttributeError: 'NoneType' object has no attribute 'tag'
```

The following patch makes this rather more friendly by instead throwing an exception describing the problem.

```
diff --git a/kmip/core/objects.py b/kmip/core/objects.py

issue_title: Feature request: registering managed Template objects
issue_body:
In KMIP it is possible to register a Template object as a distinct managed object, then request it by name as part of creating a new object using the `names` parameter of the Template Attribute list. Then all attributes in the  registered Template are included as if they were part of the request itself.

I see PyKMIP has support for reading/writing names in the `TemplateAttribute` type. So I think it is possible already to request a template be used in a create/rekey/etc call. But it is not (afaict) possible to register a new named template. Is there any possibility to add this? I tried looking around to see what is required, I see I need to derive new type from `ManagedObject` but shortly after that I kind of ran aground as I'm not sure how everything fits together.

issue_title: Separate the front-end visualization and back-end data generation for visualization from the web server
issue_body:
It would be nice to refactor both the front-end javascript and the back-end rendering code of a `VisualizationElement`, so that they aren't coupled as tightly to the Tornado server setup and its template.

## Motivation
The motivation for this issue is to allow the front-end visualizations written in javascript to be used in situations where the webserver is not needed (or wanted). Examples would include the inside of a Jupyter notebook output cell, or a static HTML page where the data for a model run could be saved statically and replayed.

## Current Code
Currently the javascript visualization code makes assumptions about the template it is loaded into,  injecting into the body tag. Some of the visualizations also send along canvas commands, rather than data which should be left to the javascript to make use of.

Each individual visualization also uses a `js_code` property to specify initialization code, which must deal with specifying arguments itself.

## Proposed Changes
- Make each visualization generate an element which can be injected into any container at the discretion of the caller. This will allow consumers like a jupyter notebook extension to inject into output cells.
- Replace `js_code` with an initialization function name e.g. `ChartModule`, and always pass a provided dictionary as the single argument containing any of the options. This will ensure new modules are written in a way which doesn't make assumptions about the type of front-end environment it will be run in and also allows the tornado webserver or notebook extension to change options as needed (possibly change dimensions?).

issue_title: Browser dies when Tornado server is killed, causes system to register application 'crash'
issue_body:
Not sure if this is desired functionality, but when the server is killed or the connection is closed **and** the visualization is the only tab in the browser, the browser is closed. Ideally this would be fine, but Chrome throws an error when you launch it independent of the server/visualization, asking to Restore as if Chrome had crashed.

This is a relatively minor issue, but it could give the user the impression that not everything is okey-dokey with their system.

issue_title: Classes should follow naming convention
issue_body:
There are quite a few classes that do not follow naming convention, i.e using plural or _s for collections and singular for resources:

e.g.
policys_s, policys ==> policys_s, policy
profiles_s, profiles ==> profiles_s, profile

There are a lot more examples, and we should refactor this to stay in line with what we set in our dev guide. This will break backwards compatibility.

Our project now uses .net core 2.0 preview, in Docker container, pushed to Microsoft azure container service with orchestration of kubernetes.

Package being used in project:

Install runtime in (VSTS) build agent machine,

2.0.0-preview2-006497
```

DockerFile:

```
FROM microsoft/dotnet:2.0.0-runtime-jessie

# set up network

When we report an no task found error. The settings property is not set and thus we try to
read guess tasks from the [`out['settings'] = obj.settings`](https://github.com/frigg/frigg-worker/blob/master/frigg_worker/jobs.py#L196), which results in this:

Could not find the file or directory at path ~/builds/<id>
```

issue_title: Make dependency on a2enmod & a2dismod optional
issue_body:
At the moment, the client makes use of a2enmod and a2dismod for enabling some mods in [configurator.py](https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/blob/master/letsencrypt-apache/letsencrypt_apache/configurator.py#L140). But these scripts are [Debian (based distro's) only](http://manpages.debian.org/cgi-bin/man.cgi?query=a2dismod). At least: those scripts are unavailable on Gentoo.

Therefore, the Apache plugin is unavailable too.

It's not unlikely the required mods are already enabled on the server. As far as I can tell, Let's Encrypt just enables [mod_ssl](https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/blob/master/letsencrypt-apache/letsencrypt_apache/configurator.py#L544), [mod_headers](https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/blob/master/letsencrypt-apache/letsencrypt_apache/configurator.py#L820) and [mod_rewrite](https://github.com/letsencrypt/letsencrypt/blob/master/letsencrypt-apache/letsencrypt_apache/configurator.py#L890). But perhaps I'm missing something :neutral_face: 

Those are not very uncommon modules at all if I'm not mistaken. I would very much like to see some kind of check: if the required modules are already properly enabled, then the dependency on a2enmod and a2dismod could be dropped.

issue_title: What stops Let's Encrypt from becoming corrupt?
issue_body:
The obvious way a CA can become corrupt is if they disclose the signing key to 3rd parties, in any way, shape or form.

So what exactly stops Let's Encrypt from such scenarios like:
- disclosing the key to the feds
- selling the key on darknet/.onion

The reason I ask is because this is a very tempting opportunity to get SSL certificates for gratis, but how can I be sure that if I don't pay for a product, am I and my traffic not the product itself?

issue_title: Create wrapper for suppression list API
Accept more than 6 digits for fractional seconds in isoparse
issue_body:
Currently, `isoparse` raises an exception if there are more than 6 digits in the fractional second.

```
>>> dateutil.parser.isoparse("2006-01-02T15:04:05.9999999-07:00")
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/home/jay/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dateutil/parser/isoparser.py", line 37, in func
    return f(self, str_in, *args, **kwargs)
  File "/home/jay/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dateutil/parser/isoparser.py", line 136, in isoparse
    components += self._parse_isotime(dt_str[pos + 1:])
  File "/home/jay/.local/lib/python2.7/site-packages/dateutil/parser/isoparser.py", line 363, in _parse_isotime
    raise ValueError('Unused components in ISO string')
ValueError: Unused components in ISO string
```

Go, for example, includes up to 9 digits (nanosecond precision) when marshaling a time into JSON. It would be nice if `isoparse` could accept those without issue, even if precision gets truncated a bit.

issue_title: General Enhancements
issue_body:
List of enhancements I've been writting down over time. They're a few. Feel free to make a pull request for any. Why not use "Projects"? Not sure, this way we can have a nice checklist!

* [ ] Should `.to_json_dict()` and `.from_json_dict()` be implemented on entities?
* [ ] Remove `from ... import ...` and encourage people to use `import telethon.tl.functions as tl` (or `from telethon.tl import functions as tl`)? Should the "Copy import" button be changed?
* [ ] Try changing the connection server on the session from whatever normal is to `149.154.167.40` (test server 2), it will crash with -404; handle these more gracefully.
* [ ] It would be nice if exceptions could be automatically generated, so people could write `except tlexception.LIMIT_INVALID` or `tlexception.ERROR_404`, or similar.
* [ ] Check better (i.e. faster) encryption modules, or fallback to pure Python ones.
* [ ] Calling `.sign_in` when you're already signed results in strange behaviour. How could this be enhanced?
* [ ] Should autocast to the right types be enabled on the generated types? If so, how?
* [ ] Make use of the temporary authorization keys. `perm auth key => create temp auth key => bind temp key to perm => session id`.
* [ ] On `BrokenAuthError`, rebind the temporary key.
* [X] Expand updates once users are saved to the database.
* [ ] Should `help.getNearestDc` be used instead excepting the first migrate error? Use both?
* [ ] `.send_media` methods should accept old messages, or file locations.
* [ ] Try sending 20 get history requests at once, and see if you get flooded.
* [ ] On reconnection, ask for updates since the last known state to get in pair.
* [ ] Documentation is generating `index#boolTrue` and `bool_true` types.
* [ ] Does `TypeNotFoundError` on connect acknowledge that it's not found so it's not re-read?
* [ ] Try sending someone's profile picture as media. Does it work?
* [ ] `.send_file` should return the media usable for `.send_media`.
* [ ] Can `.get_dialogs(limit=None)` make use of containers?
* [ ] Use generated types on the `MtProtoSender`, like pong and such.
* [ ] Mention on the wiki that hash parameters are not your API hash, use 0 instead and it should work.
* [ ] Use `input*Empty` automatically? E.g., on offsets.
* [ ] There may more places where the `seq_no` can be restarted. Where?
* [ ] Is there a need for two `MtProtoSender`'s? Can't the "plain" one use the normal one without auth key?
* [ ] Perhaps the `ReadThread` *and* main thread can be calling "receive", problem is if there's nothing to receive. The timeout is going to take longer because the background thread has actually read it, and the main is trying to read something else.
* [ ] Setting `auth_key = None` on reconnect should be at beginning of `.connect`, so if the session IP changes, we can toss auth key.
* [ ] CDNs can possibly have the same ID as normal ones, so the dictionary may be broken.
* [ ] `ConnectionReset` even when not authorized. Handle these better.
* [ ] Create the right sender when downloading media, instead creating it every time (from other thread).
* [ ] Mention in the wiki that the library saves users.
* [ ] Change int to bytes and from bytes to struct unpack on `connection.py`.
* [ ] If `auth_key is None` we need to `initConnection`, but how?
* [ ] How well will `EntityDatabase` play if several users login through the same client? The old database must be somehow invalidated. Clear it on new succesful login?
* [ ] Calling `.connect()` should not call `getState`, but rather `getDifference` or self-user.
* [ ] Make `.get_dialogs()` return a `dialogSlice` object but with all chats as needed? With the peers replaced, using the entity database which is faster than `next()`.
* [ ] Should `msgsAck` be sent less often? 150 seconds, random value?
* [ ] `EntityDatabase.__delitem__` doesn't actually work.
* [ ] Don't call `getState` if disconnected but we already know that the user is authorized on reconnect.
* [ ] Use https://readthedocs.org/
* [ ] Seems like the "tcp seq no", if invalid, the server will close the connection.
* [ ] "async sockets" with "select" (man 2 in a terminal)? Is this possible?

I couldn't find a way of embedding Html in Popups without the use of iframes. Iframes are sometimes overkill for simple nested links or browser compatability issues so I create a HTMLPopup class for element.py. Would be great if you could add this it the master branch. ``` class HTMLPopup(Element): """Create a Figure object, to plot things into it. Parameters ---------- html : str, default None Eventual HTML code that you want to put in the frame. width : str, default "100%" The width of the Figure. It may be a percentage or pixel value (like "300px"). height : str, default None The height of the Figure. It may be a percentage or a pixel value (like "300px"). ratio : str, default "60%" A percentage defining the aspect ratio of the Figure. It will be ignored if height is not None. figsize : tuple of two int, default None If you're a matplotlib addict, you can overwrite width and height. Values will be converted into pixels in using 60 dpi. For example figsize=(10, 5) will result in width="600px", height="300px". """ def __init__(self, html=None, width="100%", height=None, ratio="60%", figsize=None): super(HTMLPopup, self).__init__() self._name = 'HTMLPopup' self.width = width self.height = height self.ratio = ratio if figsize is not None: self.width = str(60*figsize[0])+'px' self.height = str(60*figsize[1])+'px' if isinstance(html, text_type) or isinstance(html, binary_type): self.add_children(Element(html)) elif html is not None: self.add_children(html) def render(self, **kwargs): """Renders the HTML representation of the element.""" html = super(HTMLPopup, self).render(**kwargs) html = html # noqa if self.height is None: htmlpopup = ( '
' '
' # noqa '{html}' '
').format htmlpopup = htmlpopup(html=html, width=self.width, ratio=self.ratio) else: htmlpopup = ('
{html}
').format htmlpopup = htmlpopup(html=html, width=self.width, height=self.height) return htmlpopup ```

issue_title: csv header parsing problem with BOM UTF8
issue_body:
I'm using Python 2.7.13 with turicreate version 4.0 shown by "pip show turicreate".

To recreate the bug, 
1. Write character 'a' to "buggy.csv" in UTF-8 encoding.

2. Run the following code:
`import turicreate as tc
data1 = tc.SFrame('buggy.csv')
print data1['a']
`

Expected output:
[]

Actual output:
RuntimeError: Column name a does not exist.

I used a debugger and found that the corresponding column in data1 for 'a' is '\xef\xbb\xbfa', which means that the BOM is incorrectly parsed as header.

issue_title: Confusion about .predict() item_id boundaries

I'm having a bit of confusion around the way to call `.predict()`, and the arguments it supports.

In the movie_lens example, the model is trained with the interaction and item_features matrices of shapes `(3221, 72360)` and `(72360, 1246)` respectively.

This leads me to assume that the model "knows" about 72360 items. Yet, when I try to predict the user-item score of user 0 with item 2000, (i.e. `model.predict(0, np.array(2000))`) I get this error:

```
assert self.item_embeddings.shape[0] >= item_features.shape[1]
```

Predicting with item_ids only works for item_ids less than 1246, which I took to be the number of item features.

Am I misinterpreting something, or is this intended behavior?

issue_title: Objective functions
issue_body:
There is currently a submodule `nntools.objectives`, but like the regularization one this was a bit of an afterthought.

I would be great if the code in `nntools.objectives` is defined in terms of Theano expressions (not `Layer` instances), so that it can be used in isolation.

But sometimes it might be convenient to have an object that represents the objective function, which defines a Theano variable for the target labels (in the case of a supervised training objective), so that you can just do something like:
```
l_out = ... # output layer of the network
obj = nntools.objectives.Objective(l_out, loss_function=nntools.objectives.mse)
loss = obj.get_loss()
```
I don't know if this warrants the extra class though. It doesn't really do that much. What does everyone think?

issue_title: Request: include data field to OptogeneticStimulusSite (ogen.py)
issue_body:
Problem/Use Case:
In case of laser scanning photostimulation (in and ex vivo) the location of the photostimulation ('OptogeneticStimulusSite' in 'ogen.py') is variable (XYZ and time). At the moment there is only the field 'Location' which allows for a string input. This suffices for a fixed stimulation location only. 

To allow for description of variable locations it would be helpful to have a data field in class('OptogeneticStimulusSite' in 'ogen.py'). The data field should allow for storage of an 4 dimensional numpy array (XYZT).

Downside of this addition of the 'Data' field would be that users without variable locations need to provide a 'Data' array. I don't know if there is away to make the 'Data' field optional, hence I haven't made a version of 'ogen.py' myself?!

issue_title: angr.sim_type.parse_type Error
issue_body:
Upon reaching a `sprintf(buf,"%lx",int)`, angr errors in the following block:
```python
try:
    typeobj = sim_type.parse_type(nugtype).with_arch(self.state.arch)
except Exception as e: #I added Exception for trace clarity
    raise SimProcedureError("%s\nformat specifier uses unknown type '%s'" % (str(e),repr(nugtype)))
```
From https://github.com/angr/angr/blob/master/angr/procedures/stubs/format_parser.py#L337
Results in:
```
<State errored with "'tuple' object has no attribute 'strip'
format specifier uses unknown type '('unsigned', 'long')'">
```
When you pass multiple format params for a single object the following line has a logic bug due to `defn` being a `tuple` instead of a `str`:
```python
defn = 'typedef ' + defn.strip('; \n\t\r') + ' QQQQ;'
```
From https://github.com/angr/angr/blob/master/angr/sim_type.py#L909

I tried patching with:
```python
if type(defn) is tuple:
    defn = ' '.join(defn)
```
But I just get a new error:
```
<State errored with "No module named ply.lex
format specifier uses unknown type '('unsigned', 'long')'">
```
And yes, `ply` is installed:
```
% ~/.local/bin/pip install ply --user
Requirement already satisfied: ply in /usr/lib/python2.7/site-packages
```

I can get it to not error by changing https://github.com/angr/angr/blob/master/angr/sim_type.py#L848
and #L849:
```python
import ply.lex as lex
import ply.cpp as cpp
```
But I have no idea what's intended.

issue_title: Maintainers for mps-youtube and pafy, and possibly moving off of GitHub
issue_body:
I took over maintaining mps-youtube from @np1 in 2015 (#259). But I haven't really been using mps-youtube (or the dependent pafy library) lately, let alone writing code for it.

I think it might be best to add some of the major contributors as collaborators, and set it so that changes have to go through pull requests and be reviewed. That way, at least one other collaborator will have to approve any changes (it looks like GitHub also allows requiring 2, 3, etc. reviews). This depends on what those contributors think of this idea.

Looking at the list of contributions, the top contributors other than me and @np1 are, in order (by number of commits), @JKatzwinkel, @kraetzin, @hrnr, @ritiek, @Razesdark, @vn-ki.

Also, we can discuss here if mps-youtube should move to another platform like GitLab. For those who aren't aware, this has suddenly become rather popular since Microsoft's acquisition of GitHub. When that happened @np1 deleted the mps-youtube and pafy repositories from GitHub, but they're restored now. I personally think a more open platform like GitLab would be better, all else being equal, but other concerns might outweigh that. I haven't really used GitLab, so I don't know how it compares. What do other contributors think?

issue_title: idiomatic way to extend paths
issue_body:
it seems `hyperlink` does not have a nice way to add path components to a base url, which is a very common operation when interacting with rest apis.

for example, consider

``` python
>>> from hyperlink import URL
>>> base_url = URL.from_text('https://example.org/api/v2')
```

let's assume i want to build a url for an endpoint below this base url, e.g. `users/search`.

this works:

``` python
>>> base_url.replace(path=base_url.path + ('users', 'search'))
URL.from_text('https://example.org/api/v2/users/search')
```

... but let's face it, this is not so nice:

- `users/search` (likely copied from some documentation) needs manual splitting into a tuple
- `base_url` is referenced twice since the `.path` tuple is required for the concatenation

making this nicer is not really possible with the current api. for example:

``` python
>>> base_url.replace(path=base_url.path + 'users/search'.split('/'))
[...]
TypeError: can only concatenate tuple (not "list") to tuple
```

oops. well, that's easy to work around:

``` python
>>> base_url.replace(path=base_url.path + tuple('users/search'.split('/')))
URL.from_text('https://example.org/api/v2/users/search')
```

...but the end result is even uglier.

it would be great if a use case like this is handled with a nicer api. thoughts?

issue_title: Test repo docker tagging
issue_body:
Per https://github.com/pulp/pulp_docker/pull/184 (https://pulp.plan.io/issues/2189), we added the ability to create or update docker tags in docker repositories. Suppose you have a busybox repo with a tag latest that points to manifest with digest `sha256:2824fe048727a69da66cf1be00cebd3bb9cfe1f238473693aa9358b411208527` and you wanted to point it to a manifest with digest `sha256:c152ddeda2b828fbb610cb9e4cb121e1879dd5301d336f0a6c070b2844a0f56d`, here's how you'd do that:

```
pulp-admin -v docker repo tag --repo-id busybox --tag-name latest --manifest-digest sha256:c152ddeda2b828fbb610cb9e4cb121e1879dd5301d336f0a6c070b2844a0f56d
```

You can also create new tags with the command like this would create a 'pulp' tag and point it to the same manifest:

```
pulp-admin -v docker repo tag --repo-id busybox --tag-name pulp --manifest-digest sha256:c152ddeda2b828fbb610cb9e4cb121e1879dd5301d336f0a6c070b2844a0f56d
```

Test Plan
---------
Given a docker repo, update an existing tag by pointing it to the manifest. 
- [ ] Validate that the tag was updated and is now pointing to the correct manifest.
- [ ] Check correct tag count in the repo ( should not change)

Given a docker repo, create a new tag and point it to a manifest. 
- [ ] Validate that the tag now exists and is pointing to the correct manifest.
- [ ] Check correct tag count in the repo ( should increase by 1)

Point a tag to a digest that doesn't have a manifest with the particular digest in pulp
- [ ] Check that an error is displayed to the user.

Point a tag to a manifest digest in another repo 
- [ ] Check that an error is displayed to the user

issue_title: snake_case field not allowed
issue_body:
```
import graphene


class Character(graphene.ObjectType):
    appears_in = graphene.List(graphene.String)

    def resolve_appears_in(self, info):
        return ["1", "2"]


class RootQuery(graphene.ObjectType):
    a = graphene.String(name=graphene.String(default_value="stranger"), y=graphene.String(default_value="xxx"))
    b = graphene.Field(lambda: Character)

    def resolve_hello(self, info, name, y):
        print info
        return 'Hello ' + name + y

    def resolve_b(self, info):
        return 123


schema = graphene.Schema(RootQuery)

result = schema.execute('{b {appears_in}}')
print {
    "data": result.data,
    "errors": result.errors
}
```
I got
```
{'errors': [GraphQLError('Cannot query field "appears_in" on type "Character". Did you mean "appearsIn"?',)], 'data': None}
```
I changed the query to `'{b {appearsIn}}'` and it works. However, GraphQL spec allows snake_cases.

It this a bug or I can config it?

issue_title: Tensorboard keras debugger feature
issue_body:
Consider Stack Overflow for getting support using TensorBoard - they have a larger community with better searchability:

https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/tensorboard

For bug reports, please include the following:

 - TensorBoard version (from pip package, also printed out when running `tensorboard`)
 - TensorFlow version if different from TensorBoard
 - OS Platform and version (e.g., Linux Ubuntu 16.04)
 - Python version (e.g. 2.7, 3.5)
 - For browser-related issues:
   - Browser type and version (e.g. Chrome 64.0.3282.140)
   - Screenshot if it's a visual issue
 
Please describe the bug as clearly as possible, and if possible provide a minimal example (code, data, and/or command line) to reproduce the issue.  Thanks!

I am unable to use the latest Tensorboard debugging feature with keras. When following the documentation posted [here](https://www.tensorflow.org/programmers_guide/debugger) and elsewhere I was instructed to insert the code snippet below:
```
import keras
from tensorflow.python import debug as tf_debug
keras.backend.set_session(
    tf_debug.LocalCLIDebugWrapperSession(tf.Session()), "<USER>:7000")


<Model fit generator stuff here>
```

Then I run the command `tensorboard --logdir=/logs/ --reload_interval=1 --debugger_port 7000` in a terminal window.

Then I run the program containing the model file and the `fit_generator` and I get this error
```
2018-04-02 15:00:01.435906: I tensorflow/core/common_runtime/gpu/gpu_device.cc:1120] Creating TensorFlow device (/device:GPU:0) -> (device: 0, name: GeForce GTX 1060, pci bus id: 0000:01:00.0, compute capability: 6.1)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "coco.py", line 505, in <module>
    layers='heads')
  File "/home/jon/Documents/Mask_RCNN/model.py", line 2296, in train
    tf_debug.LocalCLIDebugWrapperSession(tf.Session()), "jon:7000")
TypeError: set_session() takes 1 positional argument but 2 were given
```
When I simply ommit the second argument to tf_debug.LocalCLIDebugWrapperSession(), I get the terminal output of tfdgb and it doesn't forward the port to tensorboard. 

Is this a versioning issue? 
 ==== Versions === 
`Tensorboard v 1.7`
`Tensorflow v 1.4.0`
`Keras v 2.4.1`
`Python3.5`

When I look at the keras.callbacks.py file it is clear that the function set_session only contains one argument. You can see that [here](https://github.com/keras-team/keras/blob/416783156c1b07f28131c493a55a93936b5fe163/keras/backend/tensorflow_backend.py#L208)

issue_title: Thellier_GUI statistics incorrect and other issues
issue_body:
Hello,

We have been using the standalone Thellier_GUI (version 3.10.1 - Mac (app) and PC (exe)), and have found a number of problems with it, which I think need fixing.

1) We have noticed that it does not give the correct answers for dpal or dt, nor do earlier versions of the software. This is quite worrying. I think alpha maybe incorrect too - it gives a different answer to ThellierTool.

Also:

2) It does not provide all the specimen statistics when k and k' are selected as acceptance criteria - it just 'greys out' all the statistics  to the right of k or k' - and does not provide an estimate.

3) On windows: there are problems with checking items in the the SPD list and getting them to show on the same window.

Having said that I think Thellier_GUI is potentially great; I am reluctant to write another Thellier analysis tool of my own - it would just be  better if these issues were dealt with.

Adrian

Having `gcloud` with `googleapis-common-protos==1.3.5` all works fine.

Please let me know this issue should be opened in another place.

min(min(len(dataA), len(dataB)), args.train_size) // self.batch_size lr = args.lr if epoch < args.epoch_step else args.lr*(args.epoch-epoch)/(args.epoch-args.epoch_step) for idx in range(1, batch_idxs): batch_files = list(zip(dataA[idx * self.batch_size:(idx + 1) * self.batch_size], dataB[idx * self.batch_size:(idx + 1) * self.batch_size])) batch_images = [load_train_data(batch_file, args.load_size, args.fine_size) for batch_file in batch_files] batch_images = np.array(batch_images).astype(np.float32) # Update G network and record fake outputs fake_A, fake_B, _, summary_str = self.sess.run( [self.fake_A, self.fake_B, self.g_optim, self.g_sum], feed_dict={self.real_data: batch_images, self.lr: lr}) self.writer.add_summary(summary_str, counter) [fake_A, fake_B] = self.pool([fake_A, fake_B]) # Update D network _, summary_str = self.sess.run( [self.d_optim, self.d_sum], feed_dict={self.real_data: batch_images, self.fake_A_sample: fake_A, self.fake_B_sample: fake_B, self.lr: lr}) self.writer.add_summary(summary_str, counter) counter += 1 print(("Epoch: [%2d] [%4d/%4d] time: %4.4f" % ( epoch, idx, batch_idxs, time.time() - start_time))) if np.mod(counter, args.print_freq) == 1: self.sample_model(args.sample_dir, epoch, idx) if np.mod(counter, args.save_freq) == 2: self.save(args.checkpoint_dir, counter) def save(self, checkpoint_dir, step): model_name = "cyclegan.model" model_dir = "%s_%s" % (self.dataset_dir, self.image_size) checkpoint_dir = os.path.join(checkpoint_dir, model_dir) if not os.path.exists(checkpoint_dir): os.makedirs(checkpoint_dir) self.saver.save(self.sess, os.path.join(checkpoint_dir, model_name), global_step=step) def load(self, checkpoint_dir): print(" [*] Reading checkpoint...") model_dir = "%s_%s" % (self.dataset_dir, self.image_size) checkpoint_dir = os.path.join(checkpoint_dir, model_dir) ckpt = tf.train.get_checkpoint_state(checkpoint_dir) if ckpt and ckpt.model_checkpoint_path: ckpt_name = os.path.basename(ckpt.model_checkpoint_path) self.saver.restore(self.sess, os.path.join(checkpoint_dir, ckpt_name)) return True else: return False def sample_model(self, sample_dir, epoch, idx): dataA = glob('./datasets/{}/*.*'.format(self.dataset_dir + '/testA')) dataB = glob('./datasets/{}/*.*'.format(self.dataset_dir + '/testB')) np.random.shuffle(dataA) np.random.shuffle(dataB) batch_files = list(zip(dataA[:self.batch_size], dataB[:self.batch_size])) sample_images = [load_train_data(batch_file, is_testing=True) for batch_file in batch_files] sample_images = np.array(sample_images).astype(np.float32) fake_A, fake_B = self.sess.run( [self.fake_A, self.fake_B], feed_dict={self.real_data: sample_images} ) save_images(fake_A, [self.batch_size, 1], './{}/A_{:02d}_{:04d}.jpg'.format(sample_dir, epoch, idx)) save_images(fake_B, [self.batch_size, 1], './{}/B_{:02d}_{:04d}.jpg'.format(sample_dir, epoch, idx)) def test(self, args): """Test cyclegan""" init_op = tf.global_variables_initializer() self.sess.run(init_op) if args.which_direction == 'AtoB': sample_files = glob('./datasets/{}/*.*'.format(self.dataset_dir + '/testA')) elif args.which_direction == 'BtoA': sample_files = glob('./datasets/{}/*.*'.format(self.dataset_dir + '/testB')) else: raise Exception('--which_direction must be AtoB or BtoA') if self.load(args.checkpoint_dir): print(" [*] Load SUCCESS") else: print(" [!] Load failed...") # write html for visual comparison index_path = os.path.join(args.test_dir, '{0}_index.html'.format(args.which_direction)) index = open(index_path, "w") index.write("") index.write("") out_var, in_var = (self.testB, self.test_A) if args.which_direction == 'AtoB' else ( self.testA, self.test_B) for sample_file in sample_files: print('Processing image: ' + sample_file) sample_image = [load_test_data(sample_file, args.fine_size)] sample_image = np.array(sample_image).astype(np.float32) image_path = os.path.join(args.test_dir, '{0}_{1}'.format(args.which_direction, os.path.basename(sample_file))) fake_img = self.sess.run(out_var, feed_dict={in_var: sample_image}) save_images(fake_img, [1, 1], image_path) index.write("" % os.path.basename(image_path)) index.write("" % (sample_file if os.path.isabs(sample_file) else ( '..' + os.path.sep + sample_file))) index.write("" % (image_path if os.path.isabs(image_path) else ( '..' + os.path.sep + image_path))) index.write("") index.close() 2.main.py import argparse import os type=int, default=100, help='# of epoch to decay lr') parser.add_argument('--batch_size', dest='batch_size', type=int, default=1, help='# images in batch') parser.add_argument('--train_size', dest='train_size', type=int, default=1e8, help='# images used to train') parser.add_argument('--load_size', dest='load_size', type=int, default=286, help='scale images to this size') parser.add_argument('--fine_size', dest='fine_size', type=int, default=256, help='then crop to this size') parser.add_argument('--ngf', dest='ngf', type=int, default=64, help='# of gen filters in issue_title: Builders and axes
issue_body:
The following code runs with pyfftw 0.9.2 and 0.10.1 without a problem

    import numpy as np
    from pyfftw import builders

    X = np.ones((1, 33, 33, 2))

but if the `axes` argument is changed 

    import numpy as np
